340	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
341	# Deterministic conservative fallback (never a guessed contract)
342	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
343	
344	def _conservative_contract(
345	    prompt: str, candidates: list[CandidateConstraint]
346	) -> ResearchContract:
347	    """A span-verified conservative contract: raw prompt as the objective, ONLY
348	    span-supported explicit rules kept (as hard iff the candidate was hard AND has
349	    a real span), everything else open. ``compiler_degraded=true``.
350	
351	    This is the fallback when the LLM output is unusable twice — it can never
352	    invent, because it only promotes candidates that already carry a verbatim
353	    prompt span, and it downgrades any hard candidate without a span to prefer.
354	    """
355	    objective = [ContractTerm(
356	        term_id="objective.question",
357	        dimension="objective.question",
358	        value=prompt.strip(),
359	        origin=ORIGIN_EXPLICIT if prompt.strip() else ORIGIN_INFERRED,
360	        force=FORCE_OPEN,
361	        spans=[PromptSpan(0, len(prompt), prompt)] if prompt.strip() else [],
362	        rationale="degraded fallback: raw request retained as the objective",
363	    )]
364	
365	    scope: list[ContractTerm] = []
366	    coverage: list[CoverageRequirement] = []
367	    assumptions: list[Assumption] = []
368	    n = 0
369	    for cand in candidates:
370	        span_ok = any(
371	            sp.quote == prompt[sp.start:sp.end] for sp in cand.spans
372	        ) and bool(cand.spans)
373	        # A hard candidate keeps hard force ONLY with a verbatim span; otherwise
374	        # it degrades to preference (the mechanical no-invention guarantee).
375	        force = FORCE_HARD if (cand.force == "hard" and span_ok) else FORCE_PREFER
376	        origin = ORIGIN_EXPLICIT if span_ok else ORIGIN_INFERRED
377	        n += 1
378	        tid = f"scope.cand_{n}"
379	        spans = [PromptSpan(sp.start, sp.end, sp.quote) for sp in cand.spans]
380	        term = ContractTerm(
381	            term_id=tid,
382	            dimension=cand.dimension,
383	            value=cand.value,
384	            origin=origin,
385	            force=force,
386	            spans=spans,
387	            rationale=f"degraded fallback from candidate ({cand.origin})",
388	        )
389	        if cand.dimension.startswith("content"):
390	            coverage.append(CoverageRequirement(
391	                requirement_id=tid, kind="topic", statement=term, required=False,
392	            ))
393	        else:
394	            scope.append(term)
395	        if origin in DISCLOSURE_ORIGINS:
396	            assumptions.append(Assumption(
397	                assumption_id=f"asm_{n}",
398	                statement=f"kept {cand.dimension}={cand.value!r} as an open preference "
399	                          f"(no verbatim span to make it explicit)",
400	                affected_term_ids=[tid],
401	                origin=ORIGIN_INFERRED,
402	                consequence="ranked/routed only; never a hard gate",
403	            ))
404	
405	    return ResearchContract(
406	        objective=objective,
407	        scope=scope,
408	        coverage=coverage,
409	        assumptions=assumptions,
410	        complexity="degraded",
411	        compiler_degraded=True,
412	    )
413	
414	
415	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
416	# Deterministic source-scope promotion (deterministic-wins-on-overlap)
417	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
418	#
419	# THE task-72 fix. The LLM contract compiler is unreliable at retaining the
420	# source-scope rule ("high-quality, English-language journal articles"): on a real
421	# run it dropped it entirely and mis-filed "English-language" as the OUTPUT
422	# language. Meanwhile the deterministic rule-reader / intake extractors (the S0
423	# candidate adapter) find it every time, WITH verbatim prompt spans. So — exactly
424	# as ``reanchor_contract_spans`` makes the deterministic OFFSET authoritative over
425	# the LLM — this makes the deterministic SOURCE SCOPE authoritative over the LLM:
426	# span-verified source-type / source-quality / source-language candidates are
427	# PROMOTED into the contract's ``scope`` as EXPLICIT HARD terms the compiler can
428	# neither drop nor downgrade.
429	#
430	# No-invention is preserved: a candidate is promoted to force=hard ONLY when it
431	# carries a real verbatim prompt span (``quote == prompt[start:end]``). A
432	# candidate without a locatable span is skipped (never a fabricated hard gate).
433	# A candidate whose S0 force is not ``hard`` is promoted as force=preference
434	# (span-backed, explicit origin) — surfaced, never a fabricated gate.
435	#
436	# Gated behind PG_GATE (default OFF): with the flag OFF this is a no-op and the
437	# compiled contract is byte-identical to the pre-fix path.
438	
439	# Map an S0 candidate dimension onto a canonical SOURCE-scope contract dimension.
440	# ``source.scope_facet`` values like ``peer_reviewed_journal`` describe a JOURNAL
441	# source with a QUALITY bar, so a facet fires BOTH a source_types=journal_article
442	# term and a source_quality=high term (both hard when the facet was hard). This is
443	# the "English-language journal articles" → {source_types=[journal_article] hard,
444	# source_quality=high hard, source_languages=[English] hard} mapping (design §3).
445	def _promote_source_scope(
446	    contract: ResearchContract, candidates: list[CandidateConstraint], prompt: str
447	) -> ResearchContract:
448	    """Promote span-verified deterministic source-scope candidates into the
449	    contract's ``scope`` as explicit terms (hard iff the candidate was hard AND
450	    span-verified). Deterministic-wins-on-overlap: an existing scope term on the
451	    same canonical dimension+value is UPGRADED to explicit-hard rather than
452	    duplicated. Mutates + returns the contract. No-op when PG_GATE is OFF."""
453	    if not gate_enabled():
454	        return contract
455	
456	    prompt = prompt or ""
457	
458	    # A source.language candidate the intake path marks force=prefer is
459	    # nonetheless HARD when its span sits INSIDE a hard source-scope restriction
460	    # (task 72: "only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles" is a
461	    # single hard 'only' clause, so the English-language inside it is hard too).
462	    # This upgrade fires ONLY off a span-verified hard facet that verbatim
463	    # encloses the language span — never a fabricated hard gate.
464	    _hard_facet_ranges: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
465	    for _c in candidates:
466	        if (
467	            _c.dimension == "source.scope_facet"
468	            and _c.force == FORCE_HARD
469	        ):
470	            for _sp in _c.spans:
471	                if 0 <= _sp.start <= _sp.end <= len(prompt) and _sp.quote == prompt[_sp.start:_sp.end]:
472	                    _hard_facet_ranges.append((_sp.start, _sp.end))
473	
474	    def _language_is_hard(cand: CandidateConstraint) -> bool:
475	        for sp in cand.spans:
476	            for lo, hi in _hard_facet_ranges:
477	                if lo <= sp.start and sp.end <= hi:
478	                    return True
479	        return False
480	
481	    def _span_ok(cand: CandidateConstraint) -> bool:
482	        return bool(cand.spans) and any(
483	            0 <= sp.start <= sp.end <= len(prompt)
484	            and sp.quote == prompt[sp.start:sp.end]
485	            for sp in cand.spans
486	        )
487	
488	    def _spans(cand: CandidateConstraint) -> list[PromptSpan]:
489	        return [
490	            PromptSpan(sp.start, sp.end, sp.quote)
491	            for sp in cand.spans
492	            if 0 <= sp.start <= sp.end <= len(prompt)
493	            and sp.quote == prompt[sp.start:sp.end]
494	        ]
495	
496	    # (canonical scope dimension, canonical value, force) tuples to promote,
497	    # de-duplicated. A facet expands into a source_types + source_quality pair.
498	    def _targets(cand: CandidateConstraint) -> list[tuple[str, str, str]]:
499	        dim = cand.dimension
500	        val = (cand.value or "").strip()
501	        hard = cand.force == FORCE_HARD
502	        force = FORCE_HARD if hard else FORCE_PREFER
503	        out: list[tuple[str, str, str]] = []
504	        if dim == "source.types":
505	            if val == "high_quality":
506	                out.append((SCOPE_SOURCE_QUALITY, "high", force))
507	            elif val:
508	                out.append((SCOPE_SOURCE_TYPES, val, force))
509	        elif dim == "source.language":
510	            if val:
511	                lang_force = FORCE_HARD if (hard or _language_is_hard(cand)) else FORCE_PREFER
512	                out.append((SCOPE_SOURCE_LANGUAGES, val, lang_force))
513	        elif dim == "source.scope_facet":
514	            # an ontology source-type facet: journal + a quality bar.
515	            low = val.lower()
516	            if "journal" in low or "peer" in low:
517	                out.append((SCOPE_SOURCE_TYPES, "journal_article", force))
518	            if "peer" in low or "quality" in low or "high" in low:
519	                out.append((SCOPE_SOURCE_QUALITY, "high", force))
520	        return out
521	
522	    # index existing scope terms by (dimension, casefolded value) for overlap.
523	    def _key(dim: str, value: Any) -> tuple[str, str]:
524	        return (dim, str(value or "").strip().casefold())
525	
526	    existing: dict[tuple[str, str], ContractTerm] = {}
527	    for t in contract.scope:
528	        existing[_key(t.dimension, t.value)] = t
529	
530	    promoted: dict[tuple[str, str], tuple[str, list[PromptSpan]]] = {}
531	    for cand in candidates:
532	        if not _span_ok(cand):
533	            continue
534	        spans = _spans(cand)
535	        for dim, value, force in _targets(cand):
536	            key = _key(dim, value)
537	            prev = promoted.get(key)
538	            # hard wins over prefer on overlap; union the spans.
539	            if prev is None:
540	                promoted[key] = (force, list(spans))
541	            else:
542	                prev_force, prev_spans = prev
543	                new_force = FORCE_HARD if FORCE_HARD in (prev_force, force) else FORCE_PREFER
544	                seen = {(s.start, s.end) for s in prev_spans}
545	                merged = list(prev_spans) + [
546	                    s for s in spans if (s.start, s.end) not in seen
547	                ]
548	                promoted[key] = (new_force, merged)
549	
550	    idx = 0
551	    for (dim, _vfold), (force, spans) in promoted.items():
552	        idx += 1
553	        # recover the canonical (non-folded) value from the key's source targets.
554	        # We stored the folded value in the key; re-derive the display value from
555	        # the first span-owning candidate target that produced this key.
556	        value = _display_value_for(candidates, prompt, dim, _vfold)
557	        key = (dim, _vfold)
558	        term = existing.get(key)
559	        if term is not None:
560	            # overlap: UPGRADE the LLM's term to explicit + (hard if we say hard).
561	            term.origin = ORIGIN_EXPLICIT
562	            if force == FORCE_HARD:
563	                term.force = FORCE_HARD
564	            if not term.spans:
565	                term.spans = spans
566	            term.rationale = (
567	                (term.rationale + " | ") if term.rationale else ""
568	            ) + "deterministic source-scope: upheld as span-verified explicit"
569	        else:
570	            contract.scope.append(ContractTerm(
571	                term_id=f"scope.det_source_{idx}",
572	                dimension=dim,
573	                value=value,
574	                origin=ORIGIN_EXPLICIT,
575	                force=force,
576	                spans=spans,
577	                enforcement_stages=["retrieval"],
578	                rationale="deterministic source-scope promoted from the rule-reader "
579	                          "(span-verified; the LLM compiler may not drop it)",
580	            ))
581	    return contract
582	
583	
584	def _display_value_for(
585	    candidates: list[CandidateConstraint], prompt: str, dim: str, vfold: str
586	) -> Any:
587	    """Recover the canonical display value for a promoted (dim, folded-value)."""
588	    # Canonical values the promoter emits are already normalized tokens; the fold
589	    # is only for the overlap key, so map back to the un-folded canonical token.
590	    _canon = {
591	        (SCOPE_SOURCE_TYPES, "journal_article"): "journal_article",
592	        (SCOPE_SOURCE_QUALITY, "high"): "high",
593	    }
594	    if (dim, vfold) in _canon:
595	        return _canon[(dim, vfold)]
596	    # source_languages keeps the candidate's own value (e.g. "en"); find it.
597	    for cand in candidates:
598	        if cand.dimension == "source.language" and (cand.value or "").strip().casefold() == vfold:
599	            return cand.value
600	    return vfold
601	
602	
603	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
604	# Autonomous disclosure sweep (least-restrictive + every inferred term recorded)
605	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
606	
607	def _autonomous_disclose(contract: ResearchContract) -> ResearchContract:
608	    """Ensure the autonomous invariant: every inferred/policy_default term with a
609	    value has an :class:`Assumption`, and NO hard term survives without an
610	    authoritative origin (defense in depth against a slipped-through hard).
611	
612	    This mutates the contract in place and returns it. It NEVER blocks and NEVER
613	    asks — it is the pure ``contract→contract`` step that lets autonomous mode
614	    proceed with disclosed assumptions instead of questions.
615	    """
616	    disclosed = set()
617	    for a in contract.assumptions:
618	        disclosed.update(a.affected_term_ids)
619	
620	    next_idx = len(contract.assumptions)
621	    for term in contract.all_terms():
622	        # Belt: downgrade any hard term whose origin cannot back a hard force.
623	        if term.is_hard() and term.origin not in HARD_ELIGIBLE_ORIGINS:
624	            term.force = FORCE_PREFER
625	            term.rationale = (
626	                (term.rationale + " | ") if term.rationale else ""
627	            ) + "autonomous: downgraded hard→preference (non-explicit origin)"
628	
629	        if (
630	            term.origin in DISCLOSURE_ORIGINS
631	            and term.value not in (None, "", [], {})
632	            and term.term_id
633	            and term.term_id not in disclosed
634	        ):
635	            next_idx += 1
636	            contract.assumptions.append(Assumption(
637	                assumption_id=f"auto_asm_{next_idx}",
638	                statement=f"assumed {term.dimension}={term.value!r} "
639	                          f"(least-restrictive default; unspecified in the request)",
640	                affected_term_ids=[term.term_id],
641	                origin=term.origin if term.origin in DISCLOSURE_ORIGINS else ORIGIN_INFERRED,
642	                consequence="preference/routing only; never a hard gate",
643	                reversible=True,
644	            ))
645	            disclosed.add(term.term_id)
646	
647	    return contract
648	
649	
650	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
651	# Ask-vs-assume (interactive only; ≤3 material questions)
652	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
653	
654	def _material_questions(contract: ResearchContract, limit: int = 3) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
655	    """Generate ≤``limit`` material questions from the contract's ambiguities.
656	
657	    Eligible only if the ambiguity is marked material AND cannot proceed open.
658	    Autonomous mode NEVER calls this. Coupled ambiguities are de-duplicated by
659	    their affected term set.
660	    """
661	    seen_keys: set[tuple[str, ...]] = set()
662	    out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
663	    for amb in contract.ambiguities:
664	        if not amb.material or amb.can_proceed_open:
665	            continue
666	        key = tuple(sorted(amb.affected_term_ids))
667	        if key in seen_keys:
668	            continue
669	        seen_keys.add(key)
670	        out.append({
671	            "question_id": f"q_{len(out) + 1}",
672	            "prompt": amb.text,
673	            "choices": list(amb.plausible_interpretations) + ["keep broad / use best judgment"],
674	            "affected_term_ids": list(amb.affected_term_ids),
675	            "why_it_matters": ", ".join(amb.decision_impact) or "materially changes the plan",
676	        })
677	        if len(out) >= limit:
678	            break
679	    return out
680	
681	
682	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
683	# LLM call plumbing
684	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
685	
686	async def _call(
687	    client: Any, system: str, user: str, max_tokens: int
688	) -> str:
689	    # I-gate-089/FX-01: bound the reasoning-first pool so a fixed content slice
690	    # (max_tokens - pool) always survives — otherwise glm-5.2's effort=high
691	    # reasoning prelude eats the whole budget and content truncates at
692	    # finish_reason='length'. Cap the pool at half the budget so content never
693	    # loses; glm-5.2's branch-1 honors reasoning.max_tokens. Passed positionally-
694	    # safe as a kwarg so the OFF/stub paths (which ignore **kwargs) stay identical.
695	    _reasoning_pool = min(_PLANNING_GATE_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS, max(max_tokens // 2, 1))
696	    response = await client.generate(
697	        prompt=user,
698	        system=system,
699	        max_tokens=max_tokens,
700	        temperature=0.0,
701	        reasoning_max_tokens=_reasoning_pool,
702	    )
703	    return getattr(response, "content", None) or ""
704	
705	
706	def _errors_block(errors: list[ValidationError]) -> str:
707	    return json.dumps([e.to_dict() for e in errors], ensure_ascii=False)
708	
709	
710	# Validator error codes that are REPAIRABLE by the autonomous disclosure sweep
711	# (or are advisory), so they must NOT force the conservative fallback / discard a
712	# good contract. Everything else (the no-invention + span invariants: bad enums,
713	# hard_not_explicit, explicit_without_span, span_quote_mismatch, duplicate ids)
714	# is FATAL and drives the retry → fallback path.
715	_REPAIRABLE_CODES: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
716	    "inferred_not_disclosed",
717	    "mandatory_lane_count_mismatch",
718	})
719	
720	
721	# In AUTONOMOUS mode an inferred-hard that survives the retry is not fatal: the
722	# disclosure sweep downgrades it hard→preference (never a fabricated hard gate),
723	# so it must not nuke an otherwise-good contract to the raw-prompt fallback. In
724	# interactive mode it stays fatal (a human can fix it). The span-integrity checks
725	# stay fatal in BOTH modes — a bad span can never be silently kept.
726	_AUTONOMOUS_REPAIRABLE_CODES: frozenset[str] = _REPAIRABLE_CODES | frozenset({
727	    "hard_not_explicit",
728	})
729	
730	
731	def _fatal_errors(
732	    errors: list[ValidationError], *, mode: str = "interactive"
733	) -> list[ValidationError]:
734	    repairable = (
735	        _AUTONOMOUS_REPAIRABLE_CODES if mode == "autonomous" else _REPAIRABLE_CODES
736	    )
737	    return [e for e in errors if e.code not in repairable]
738	
739	
740	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
741	# The gate entry point
742	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
743	
744	async def run_research_planning_gate(
745	    prompt: str,
746	    *,
747	    mode: str,
748	    client: Any = None,
749	    rule_reader: Any = None,
750	    ontology: Any = None,
751	    run_id: Optional[str] = None,
752	    model: Optional[str] = None,
753	) -> GateResult:
754	    """Compile ``prompt`` into a pinned :class:`PlanningGateArtifact`.
755	
756	    Parameters
757	    ----------
758	    prompt:
759	        The raw task prompt.
760	    mode:
761	        ``"autonomous"`` (benchmark — never blocks) or ``"interactive"`` (may
762	        return ``needs_input`` with ≤3 material questions). The caller passes it
763	        EXPLICITLY; the gate never infers mode from a terminal/timeout.
764	    client:
765	        An LLM client with an async ``generate(prompt, system, max_tokens,
766	        temperature)`` returning an object with ``.content``. Tests inject a
767	        stub. When ``None``, a real :class:`OpenRouterClient` is built ONLY if
768	        ``PG_PLANNING_GATE_LIVE=1``; otherwise this raises (the OFF path never
769	        fires an LLM).
770	    rule_reader / ontology:
771	        Forwarded to the S0 ``reconcile_candidates`` candidate seeding.
772	
773	    Returns
774	    -------
775	    GateResult
776	        Autonomous: ``state`` is ``auto_pinned`` (or ``unsatisfiable`` on a fatal
777	        explicit contradiction), ``needs_input`` ALWAYS False. Interactive:
778	        ``needs_input`` True iff there are material questions, else ``approved``.
779	    """
780	    mode = (mode or "").strip().lower()
781	    if mode not in ("interactive", "autonomous"):
782	        raise ValueError(
783	            f"mode must be 'interactive' or 'autonomous', got {mode!r} "
784	            f"(the caller passes it explicitly; the gate never infers it)"
785	        )
786	
787	    if client is None:
788	        if not _live_enabled():
789	            raise RuntimeError(
790	                "research planning gate: live LLM disabled. Set "
791	                "PG_PLANNING_GATE_LIVE=1 for a real call, or pass client= in tests."
792	            )
793	        from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (  # noqa: PLC0415
794	            OpenRouterClient,
795	        )
796	        client = OpenRouterClient(model=model or _resolve_model())
797	
798	    run_id = run_id or f"gate_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"
799	
800	    # --- 1. deterministic candidate seeding (S0 adapter) ---
801	    try:
802	        candidates = reconcile_candidates(
803	            prompt, rule_reader=rule_reader, ontology=ontology
804	        )
805	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — candidates are advisory; fail-open to none
806	        candidates = []
807	
808	    # --- 2. contract compile (+ one correction retry, then fallback) ---
809	    contract, contract_errors, degraded = await _compile_contract(
810	        client, prompt, candidates, mode=mode
811	    )
812	
813	    # --- 2b. deterministic source-scope promotion (the task-72 fix) ---
814	    # The LLM compiler cannot be trusted to retain the source-scope rule; the
815	    # deterministic rule-reader finds it every time with verbatim spans. Promote
816	    # those span-verified source-type/quality/language candidates into the
817	    # contract as EXPLICIT HARD terms so the LLM can neither drop nor misfile
818	    # them. No-op (byte-identical) when PG_GATE is OFF.
819	    contract = _promote_source_scope(contract, candidates, prompt)
820	    contract_errors = validate_contract(contract, prompt)
821	
822	    # --- 3. mode split (the ONLY node the two modes differ at) ---
823	    if mode == "autonomous":
824	        contract = _autonomous_disclose(contract)
825	        # re-validate after disclosure (should be clean; record if not)
826	        contract_errors = validate_contract(contract, prompt)
827	        fatal = any(c.fatal for c in contract.conflicts)
828	        state = "unsatisfiable" if fatal else "auto_pinned"
829	        questions: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
830	        needs_input = False
831	    else:
832	        questions = _material_questions(contract)
833	        needs_input = bool(questions)
834	        state = "needs_input" if needs_input else "approved"
835	
836	    # --- 4. plan compile (from the validated contract) ---
837	    plan, plan_errors = await _compile_plan(client, contract)
838	
839	    artifact = PlanningGateArtifact(
840	        run_id=run_id,
841	        mode=mode,
842	        state=state,
843	        original_prompt=prompt,
844	        contract=contract,
845	        plan=plan,
846	        created_at=_dt.datetime.now(_dt.timezone.utc).isoformat(),
847	        approval_actor="autonomous_policy" if mode == "autonomous" else None,
848	        approval_policy_version="planning-gate/1.0" if mode == "autonomous" else None,
849	    )
850	    for q in questions:
851	        from src.polaris_graph.planning.planning_gate_schema import (  # noqa: PLC0415
852	            ClarificationQuestion,
853	        )
854	        artifact.clarification_questions.append(ClarificationQuestion(
855	            question_id=q["question_id"],
856	            prompt=q["prompt"],
857	            choices=q["choices"],
858	            affected_term_ids=q["affected_term_ids"],
859	            why_it_matters=q["why_it_matters"],
860	        ))
861	    artifact.recompute_hashes()
862	    if degraded:
863	        artifact.contract.compiler_degraded = True
864	
865	    return GateResult(
866	        artifact=artifact,
867	        needs_input=needs_input,
868	        questions=questions,
869	        contract_errors=contract_errors,
870	        plan_errors=plan_errors,
871	    )
872	
873	
874	async def _compile_contract(
875	    client: Any,
876	    prompt: str,
877	    candidates: list[CandidateConstraint],
878	    *,
879	    mode: str = "interactive",
880	) -> tuple[ResearchContract, list[ValidationError], bool]:
881	    """Contract compile with one bounded correction retry, then conservative
882	    fallback. Returns ``(contract, remaining_errors, degraded)``.
883	
884	    The retry fires on ANY validation error (push the compiler to fix even
885	    repairable ones). The final keep-vs-fallback decision uses the MODE-AWARE
886	    fatal set: in autonomous mode an inferred-hard the compiler wouldn't fix is
887	    downgraded by the disclosure sweep rather than nuking the whole contract to
888	    the raw-prompt fallback; span-integrity violations stay fatal in both modes.
889	    """
890	    user = _contract_user_prompt(prompt, candidates, mode="compile")
891	    try:
892	        raw = await _call(client, _CONTRACT_SYSTEM_PROMPT, user, _CONTRACT_MAX_TOKENS)
893	        data = _loads(raw)
894	        contract = contract_from_dict(data.get("contract", data))
895	        # The LLM copies the verbatim quote correctly but drifts on character
896	        # offsets; re-derive each span from its quote (no-invention: a quote not
897	        # present in the prompt is left to fail validation). Mirrors the S0
898	        # candidate adapter's _locate_span discipline.
899	        reanchor_contract_spans(contract, prompt)
900	        errors = validate_contract(contract, prompt)
901	        if not _fatal_errors(errors, mode=mode):
902	            # No mode-fatal violation. Any remaining errors are repairable (an
903	            # undisclosed inferred term the autonomous sweep backfills, or an
904	            # inferred-hard the sweep downgrades), so keep the good contract.
905	            return contract, errors, False
906	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
907	        logger.warning("contract compile failed (attempt 1): %s", exc)
908	        errors = [ValidationError("compile_exception", str(exc))]
909	        contract = None  # type: ignore[assignment]
910	
911	    # --- one correction retry with machine-readable errors ---
912	    retry_user = (
913	        user
914	        + "\n\nYour previous output was rejected by the deterministic validator "
915	          "with these errors. Fix EVERY one — especially any hard term that is "
916	          "not origin=explicit, and any explicit term whose span does not quote "
917	          "the request exactly. Return corrected JSON only.\n"
918	        + _errors_block(errors)
919	    )
920	    try:
921	        raw2 = await _call(client, _CONTRACT_SYSTEM_PROMPT, retry_user, _CONTRACT_MAX_TOKENS)
922	        data2 = _loads(raw2)
923	        contract2 = contract_from_dict(data2.get("contract", data2))
924	        reanchor_contract_spans(contract2, prompt)
925	        errors2 = validate_contract(contract2, prompt)
926	        if not _fatal_errors(errors2, mode=mode):
927	            return contract2, errors2, False
928	        # retry still has a mode-fatal violation -> conservative fallback
929	        logger.warning(
930	            "contract compile still invalid after retry: %d fatal errors",
931	            len(_fatal_errors(errors2, mode=mode)),
932	        )
933	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
934	        logger.warning("contract compile failed (attempt 2): %s", exc)
935	
936	    fallback = _conservative_contract(prompt, candidates)
937	    return fallback, validate_contract(fallback, prompt), True
938	
939	
940	async def _compile_plan(
941	    client: Any, contract: ResearchContract
942	) -> tuple[ResearchExecutionPlan, list[ValidationError]]:
943	    """Plan compile with one bounded correction retry, then a deterministic
944	    one-thread-per-required-clause fallback (never drop a clause)."""
945	    user = _plan_user_prompt(contract)
946	    try:
947	        raw = await _call(client, _PLAN_SYSTEM_PROMPT, user, _PLAN_MAX_TOKENS)
948	        data = _loads(raw)
949	        plan = plan_from_dict(data.get("plan", data))
950	        errors = validate_plan(plan, contract)
951	        if not errors:
952	            return plan, []
953	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
954	        logger.warning("plan compile failed (attempt 1): %s", exc)
955	        errors = [ValidationError("compile_exception", str(exc))]
956	        plan = None  # type: ignore[assignment]
957	
958	    retry_user = (
959	        user
960	        + "\n\nYour previous plan was rejected by the validator with these "
961	          "errors. Fix EVERY one — give every required coverage requirement a "
962	          "mandatory query intent via a thread, and bind every hard contract "
963	          "term in coverage_matrix. Return corrected JSON only.\n"
964	        + _errors_block(errors)
965	    )
966	    try:
967	        raw2 = await _call(client, _PLAN_SYSTEM_PROMPT, retry_user, _PLAN_MAX_TOKENS)
968	        data2 = _loads(raw2)
969	        plan2 = plan_from_dict(data2.get("plan", data2))
970	        errors2 = validate_plan(plan2, contract)
971	        if not errors2:
972	            return plan2, []
973	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
974	        logger.warning("plan compile failed (attempt 2): %s", exc)
975	
976	    return _fallback_plan(contract), []
977	
978	
979	def _fallback_plan(contract: ResearchContract) -> ResearchExecutionPlan:
980	    """Deterministic one-thread-per-required-clause plan — never drops a clause."""
981	    from src.polaris_graph.planning.planning_gate_schema import (  # noqa: PLC0415
982	        BudgetEnvelope,
983	        CoverageBinding,
984	        QueryIntent,
985	        ResearchThread,
986	    )
987	
988	    threads: list[ResearchThread] = []
989	    intents: list[QueryIntent] = []
990	    matrix: list[CoverageBinding] = []
991	
992	    reqs = [c for c in contract.coverage if c.required] or contract.coverage
993	    if not reqs:
994	        # a narrow prompt with no explicit coverage: one discovery thread on the
995	        # objective (never fabricate facets).
996	        obj_val = ""
997	        for t in contract.objective:
998	            if t.value:
999	                obj_val = str(t.value)
1000	                break
1001	        threads.append(ResearchThread(
1002	            thread_id="t1", question=obj_val, purpose="objective discovery",
1003	            mandatory=True,
1004	        ))
1005	        intents.append(QueryIntent(
1006	            intent_id="qi1", thread_id="t1", purpose="discovery",
1007	            concepts=[obj_val] if obj_val else [], mandatory=True,
1008	        ))
1009	    else:
1010	        for i, cr in enumerate(reqs, 1):
1011	            tid = f"t{i}"
1012	            qid = f"qi{i}"
1013	            stmt = str(cr.statement.value or cr.requirement_id or f"requirement {i}")
1014	            threads.append(ResearchThread(
1015	                thread_id=tid, question=stmt, purpose="cover requirement",
1016	                coverage_requirement_ids=[cr.requirement_id] if cr.requirement_id else [],
1017	                mandatory=True,
1018	            ))
1019	            intents.append(QueryIntent(
1020	                intent_id=qid, thread_id=tid, purpose="discovery",
1021	                concepts=[stmt], mandatory=True,
1022	            ))
1023	
1024	    for term in contract.hard_terms():
1025	        if term.term_id:
1026	            matrix.append(CoverageBinding(
1027	                contract_term_id=term.term_id,
1028	                owning_stages=["retrieval"],
1029	                audit_method="deterministic",
1030	            ))
1031	
1032	    return ResearchExecutionPlan(
1033	        threads=threads,
1034	        query_intents=intents,
1035	        coverage_matrix=matrix,
1036	        budget=BudgetEnvelope(
1037	            mandatory_lane_count=len(intents),
1038	            overflow_policy="expand",
1039	        ),
1040	        stop_conditions=["every mandatory thread has usable evidence of the required kind"],
1041	    )
1042

"""Research Planning Gate compiler (S1) — contract + plan, OFFLINE-testable.

Turns a raw task prompt into a pinned :class:`PlanningGateArtifact` via two
bounded structured LLM calls:

  1. **contract compiler** — deterministic candidates (S0 adapter) + the raw
     prompt → a typed :class:`ResearchContract` (every clause dispositioned,
     every term tagged origin/force/spans);
  2. **plan compiler** — the *validated* contract → a
     :class:`ResearchExecutionPlan` (threads, mandatory query intents, outline
     seed, coverage matrix, budget).

Both calls go through the repo LLM gateway (:class:`OpenRouterClient`) resolved
to the small policy model (``z-ai/glm-5.2``, the same arm FS query-gen uses).
The live call is OFF by default: the whole module is spend-free and hermetic
unless a flag is set OR a ``client=`` stub is injected (tests do the latter).

Guardrail posture
-----------------
* **Default-OFF / None-default.** ``run_research_planning_gate`` requires an
  explicit ``client`` (a stub in tests) OR the live flag
  ``PG_PLANNING_GATE_LIVE=1``. With neither, and no client, it raises rather
  than silently hitting the network — the OFF path never fires an LLM.
* **Autonomous mode is pure ``contract→contract``.** It NEVER returns
  ``needs_input``, never blocks, never waits for a human: every would-ask becomes
  a disclosed :class:`Assumption` and the artifact is ``auto_pinned``. This is
  the load-bearing benchmark mode (a test asserts zero blocking with no input
  channel).
* **No invention.** The compiler system prompt forbids inventing constraints and
  requires spans for explicit terms; the deterministic
  :func:`~planning_gate_schema.validate_contract` then MECHANICALLY rejects any
  hard term that is not explicit/user-backed. A rejected contract gets ONE
  correction retry, then a conservative span-verified fallback
  (``compiler_degraded=true``) — never a guessed contract, never a crash.
* **Reuse.** Candidate seeding reuses the S0 ``candidate_adapter``; parsing
  reuses the fence-tolerant discipline of ``constraint_extractor`` /
  ``research_planner``; hashing reuses ``planning_gate_schema.sha256_of`` (same
  construction as ``research_planner.plan_sha256``).

Nothing here is wired into the paid pipeline — that is S2+. This module is the
compiler + its offline harness.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import datetime as _dt
import json
import logging
import os
import uuid
from typing import Any, Optional

from src.polaris_graph.planning.candidate_adapter import (
    CandidateConstraint,
    reconcile_candidates,
)
from src.polaris_graph.planning.gate_flags import gate_enabled
from src.polaris_graph.planning.planning_gate_schema import (
    DISCLOSURE_ORIGINS,
    FORCE_HARD,
    FORCE_OPEN,
    FORCE_PREFER,
    HARD_ELIGIBLE_ORIGINS,
    ORIGIN_EXPLICIT,
    ORIGIN_INFERRED,
    ORIGIN_POLICY_DEFAULT,
    SCOPE_SOURCE_LANGUAGES,
    SCOPE_SOURCE_QUALITY,
    SCOPE_SOURCE_TYPES,
    Assumption,
    ContractTerm,
    CoverageRequirement,
    PlanningGateArtifact,
    PromptSpan,
    ResearchContract,
    ResearchExecutionPlan,
    ValidationError,
    contract_from_dict,
    plan_from_dict,
    reanchor_contract_spans,
    sha256_of,
    validate_contract,
    validate_plan,
)

logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.research_planning_gate")

# The small policy model — the same arm FS query-gen / the judges use. Overridable
# via env so it tracks the lock rather than pinning a slug that could drift.
_DEFAULT_POLICY_MODEL = "z-ai/glm-5.2"

# I-gate-089 / FX-01 (drb_72 live probe): the policy model (z-ai/glm-5.2) is
# REASONING-FIRST — it is in openrouter_client._ALWAYS_REASON_MODELS, so a
# generate() call runs its reasoning prelude at effort=high on the SAME
# overall max_tokens budget and only floors that budget to PG_GLM5_MIN_MAX_TOKENS
# (4096). On a compound prompt (task 72) the reasoning prelude ate the whole
# 8192 budget: candidate content reached 14022 then 16546 chars and STILL hit
# finish_reason='length' (always_reason_promotion truncation), so the compile
# retried and fell to the conservative fallback. Sol's design (§4) says the
# ~2000-token cap is inadequate and to use the PROVIDER-RESOLVED MAXIMUM for a
# reasoning-first model. Mirror the champion's reasoning-first budget convention
# (multi_section_generator / analyst_synthesis): give the compile a real content
# ceiling AND bound the reasoning POOL so a fixed content slice always survives.
# The client further clamps DOWN to the provider completion cap (B10 resolver),
# so this can never over-request. Env-tunable; the fail-soft conservative-contract
# fallback stays the backstop when the compile still fails.
_CONTRACT_MAX_TOKENS = int(
    os.getenv("PG_PLANNING_GATE_MAX_TOKENS", "32768")
)
_PLAN_MAX_TOKENS = int(
    os.getenv("PG_PLANNING_GATE_MAX_TOKENS", "32768")
)
# Bound the reasoning-first pool so the model always reaches the closing JSON:
# reserve the remainder of the budget for content. glm-5.2 branch-1 honors a
# caller-passed reasoning.max_tokens (openrouter_client ~line 1958), so this
# guarantees content headroom = max_tokens - this pool. Env-tunable (LAW VI).
_PLANNING_GATE_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS = int(
    os.getenv("PG_PLANNING_GATE_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS", "16384")
)


def _resolve_model() -> str:
    override = os.getenv("PG_PLANNING_GATE_MODEL", "").strip()
    if override:
        return override
    policy = os.getenv("PG_POLICY_MODEL", "").strip()
    return policy or _DEFAULT_POLICY_MODEL


def _live_enabled() -> bool:
    """Whether a live LLM call is permitted. Default OFF (hermetic)."""
    return os.getenv("PG_PLANNING_GATE_LIVE", "0").strip().lower() in (
        "1", "true", "yes", "on",
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# System prompts (design §3 / §4 — no-invention discipline is IN the prompt AND
# re-checked by the deterministic validators)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

    "9. Do not set source-count, date-window, jurisdiction, length, or "
