299	    # -- the mechanized 997-guard (planning level) ----------------------------
300	
301	    def candidate_query_count(self, *, base_question: str = "") -> int:
302	        """Count the candidate query/evidence lanes this projection ADDS.
303	
304	        The 997-guard: a caller asserts ``gate_count >= no_gate_count`` so the
305	        gate path can NEVER reduce the candidate query/evidence-need count vs the
306	        no-gate path — at PLANNING level, before any fetch. Counts distinct
307	        amplified queries plus routed evidence needs (each a discovery lane)."""
308	        return (
309	            len(self.to_amplified_queries(base_question=base_question))
310	            + len(self.evidence_needs)
311	        )
312	
313	
314	def _safe_need(n: Any) -> bool:
315	    from src.polaris_graph.planning.research_planner import EVIDENCE_NEEDS  # noqa: PLC0415
316	    return _norm(n) in EVIDENCE_NEEDS
317	
318	
319	def _intent_query_texts(qi: QueryIntent) -> list[str]:
320	    """The query string(s) a single :class:`QueryIntent` contributes.
321	
322	    Prefers a concept+required-term join; falls back to alternate phrasings, then
323	    the raw thread question via concepts. Always returns at least one non-empty
324	    string when the intent carries any text."""
325	    parts: list[str] = []
326	    core = " ".join([*qi.concepts, *qi.required_terms]).strip()
327	    if core:
328	        parts.append(core)
329	    for alt in qi.alternate_phrasings:
330	        alt = (alt or "").strip()
331	        if alt:
332	            parts.append(alt)
333	    if not parts:
334	        # nothing structured — fall back to any single concept/phrasing.
335	        for cand in [*qi.concepts, *qi.alternate_phrasings]:
336	            if (cand or "").strip():
337	                parts.append(cand.strip())
338	                break
339	    return parts
340	
341	
342	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
343	# Compilation from a pinned artifact / contract
344	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
345	
346	# Scope dimensions whose VALUES are folded into query text as scope terms.
347	_SCOPE_TEXT_DIMENSIONS: tuple[str, ...] = (
348	    "scope.source_types",
349	    "scope.source_quality",  # high-quality/peer-reviewed steers the citable menu
350	    "scope.domains",
351	    "scope.geographies",
352	    "scope.prohibited",  # a prohibition is disclosed as a term the caller may filter
353	)
354	_LANGUAGE_DIMENSIONS: tuple[str, ...] = (
355	    "scope.source_languages",
356	    "scope.languages",
357	)
358	
359	
360	def from_contract_and_plan(
361	    contract: ResearchContract,
362	    plan: ResearchExecutionPlan,
363	    *,
364	    original_prompt: str = "",
365	) -> RetrievalProjection:
366	    """Compile a :class:`RetrievalProjection` from a contract + plan.
367	
368	    Pure + deterministic. Walks the contract's scope + content terms and the
369	    plan's query intents to derive routing tokens. Fail-open: a degraded/empty
370	    contract yields an empty projection (the caller keeps the champion path).
371	    """
372	    evidence_needs: list[str] = []
373	    jurisdictions: list[str] = []
374	    entities: list[str] = []
375	    comparators: list[str] = []
376	    hard_scope_terms: list[str] = []
377	    soft_scope_terms: list[str] = []
378	    hard_languages: list[str] = []
379	
380	    def _dedup_add(lst: list[str], val: Any) -> None:
381	        s = str(val or "").strip()
382	        if not s:
383	            return
384	        if s.lower() not in {x.lower() for x in lst}:
385	            lst.append(s)
386	
387	    for term in contract.scope:
388	        dim = _norm(term.dimension)
389	        val = term.value
390	        if val in (None, "", [], {}):
391	            continue
392	        vals = val if isinstance(val, (list, tuple)) else [val]
393	
394	        if dim in _LANGUAGE_DIMENSIONS:
395	            if term.force == FORCE_HARD:
396	                for v in vals:
397	                    _dedup_add(hard_languages, v)
398	            continue
399	
400	        # source_types route evidence needs (the GO-FIND-journals map).
401	        if dim == "scope.source_types":
402	            for v in vals:
403	                need = source_type_to_need(v)
404	                if need:
405	                    _dedup_add(evidence_needs, need)
406	
407	        if dim in ("scope.jurisdictions", "scope.geographies"):
408	            for v in vals:
409	                _dedup_add(jurisdictions, str(v).strip().upper()[:4])
410	
411	        # fold the value into the hard/soft scope text bucket.
412	        bucket = hard_scope_terms if term.force == FORCE_HARD else soft_scope_terms
413	        if dim in _SCOPE_TEXT_DIMENSIONS or term.force == FORCE_HARD:
414	            for v in vals:
415	                _dedup_add(bucket, v)
416	
417	    # entities + comparators from the content spec (sub-entity discovery breadth).
418	    for ent in contract.content_terms:
419	        if _norm(ent.dimension).startswith("content.entit") and ent.value:
420	            _dedup_add(entities, ent.value)
421	    for cov in contract.coverage:
422	        if cov.kind in ("entity",) and cov.statement.value:
423	            _dedup_add(entities, cov.statement.value)
424	        if cov.kind in ("comparison",):
425	            for c in cov.compare_on:
426	                if c.value:
427	                    _dedup_add(comparators, c.value)
428	
429	    return RetrievalProjection(
430	        contract=contract,
431	        plan=plan,
432	        original_prompt=original_prompt,
433	        evidence_needs=evidence_needs,
434	        jurisdictions=jurisdictions,
435	        entities=entities,
436	        comparators=comparators,
437	        hard_scope_terms=hard_scope_terms,
438	        soft_scope_terms=soft_scope_terms,
439	        hard_languages=hard_languages,
440	    )
441	
442	
443	def from_artifact(artifact: PlanningGateArtifact) -> RetrievalProjection:
444	    """Compile a :class:`RetrievalProjection` from a pinned artifact."""
445	    return from_contract_and_plan(
446	        artifact.contract,
447	        artifact.plan,
448	        original_prompt=artifact.original_prompt,
449	    )
450	
451	
452	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
453	# Transitional adapter: champion ResearchPlan -> projection (the FS seam fix)
454	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
455	
456	
457	class _ChampionPlanProjection(RetrievalProjection):
458	    """A projection whose amplified queries ARE the champion plan's ``sub_queries``.
459	
460	    S2 seam fix (design §5): the FS-Researcher branch in ``run_one_query``
461	    currently passes only ``_clean_question`` and DISCARDS
462	    ``_research_plan.sub_queries``. This adapter wraps a champion
463	    ``research_planner.ResearchPlan`` as a :class:`RetrievalProjection` so the FS
464	    frontier is seeded with those sub-queries (the gate's pre-retrieval lanes)
465	    plus the frame's ``evidence_needs`` routing — BEFORE any fetch. Until the full
466	    gate artifact is wired into ``run_one_query`` (a later step), this carries the
467	    already-computed champion plan into the FS query frontier instead of throwing
468	    it away. It NEVER drops a source; it only ADDS the sub-queries the FS branch
469	    was discarding, and only when ``PG_GATE`` is ON.
470	    """
471	
472	    def __init__(self, research_plan: Any, *, original_prompt: str = "") -> None:
473	        frame = getattr(research_plan, "frame", None)
474	        sub_queries = list(getattr(research_plan, "sub_queries", []) or [])
475	        super().__init__(
476	            contract=ResearchContract(),
477	            plan=ResearchExecutionPlan(),
478	            original_prompt=original_prompt,
479	            evidence_needs=list(getattr(frame, "evidence_needs", []) or []),
480	            jurisdictions=list(getattr(frame, "jurisdictions", []) or []),
481	            entities=list(getattr(frame, "entities", []) or []),
482	            comparators=list(getattr(frame, "comparators", []) or []),
483	        )
484	        self._sub_queries = sub_queries
485	        self._frame = frame
486	
487	    def to_amplified_queries(self, *, base_question: str = "") -> list[str]:
488	        out: list[str] = []
489	        seen: set[str] = set()
490	        for q in self._sub_queries:
491	            qs = (q or "").strip()
492	            if qs and qs.lower() not in seen:
493	                seen.add(qs.lower())
494	                out.append(qs)
495	        return out
496	
497	    def to_research_frame(self) -> Any:
498	        return self._frame
499	
500	    def to_protocol(self, *, base_question: str = "") -> Optional[dict[str, Any]]:
501	        if self._frame is None:
502	            return None
503	        try:
504	            return self._frame.to_anchor_protocol(
505	                (base_question or self.original_prompt or "").strip()
506	            )
507	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
508	            return None
509	
510	
511	def from_champion_plan(
512	    research_plan: Any, *, original_prompt: str = ""
513	) -> Optional[RetrievalProjection]:
514	    """Adapt a champion ``ResearchPlan`` into a projection, or ``None`` if it has
515	    no usable sub-queries (keep the champion FS path)."""
516	    if research_plan is None:
517	        return None
518	    if not list(getattr(research_plan, "sub_queries", []) or []):
519	        return None
520	    return _ChampionPlanProjection(research_plan, original_prompt=original_prompt)
521

file.content:
"""Retrieval projection (S2) — the no-starvation core, compile-only + offline.

This is the typed *retrieval view* of a pinned :class:`PlanningGateArtifact`
(contract + plan). It is the S2 lever the consolidated design (§5, §4 retrieval
row) calls out: the gate's scope must reach FS-Researcher **query generation**
and backend **routing** BEFORE the first fetch — never as a post-fetch filter of
a frozen corpus (the banned 997->131 anti-pattern).

A :class:`RetrievalProjection` COMPILES the contract+plan into exactly the three
levers ``live_retriever.run_live_retrieval`` already exposes but the driver never
populates from the gate:

  * ``research_frame``  — a :class:`research_planner.ResearchFrame` whose
    ``evidence_needs`` are ROUTED from the contract's ``source_types`` (journal /
    academic / scholarly -> ``primary_literature`` = *GO FIND journals*, routing
    the S2 / OpenAlex scholarly adapters), plus entities / comparators /
    jurisdictions for the field-agnostic need-type registry;
  * ``amplified_queries`` — query TEXT built from the plan's MANDATORY query
    intents: concepts + required-terms, plus native-language sub-queries for a
    hard ``source_languages`` term, plus per-entity sub-queries, plus the hard
    scope terms folded into the query text so a "journal-only" rule shapes
    discovery from the first query;
  * ``protocol`` — the frame's anchor protocol (``to_anchor_protocol``), so the
    amplified sub-queries validate against the frame's tokens.

Guardrail posture
-----------------
* **Compile-only. No network, no LLM, no I/O.** This module builds plain data
  (lists of strings, a dataclass) from an already-pinned artifact. It NEVER
  fetches. The only cross-module import is the champion ``ResearchFrame`` (a pure
  dataclass) which it constructs; that construction runs the frame's own
  ``validate_evidence_needs`` so a routed need is always a valid enum member.
* **ADD / ROUTE, never filter.** Every method returns queries or routing hints
  that ENLARGE or STEER discovery. There is no drop path here. The mechanized
  997-guard (:func:`candidate_query_count`) counts the candidate query/evidence
  lanes so a caller can assert the gate path never REDUCES the lane count vs the
  no-gate path — at planning level, before any fetch.
* **Hard vs soft.** A HARD scope term (``force==hard``, which the schema
  guarantees is explicit/user-backed) is folded into query TEXT and routed to a
  backend need. A SOFT term (preference/open) contributes ranking/entity breadth
  but is NEVER allowed to gate. This module cannot introduce a hard filter — it
  only emits query strings + need tokens.
* **Fail-open.** Every builder tolerates a degraded/empty contract: an empty
  contract yields an empty projection (no needs, no amplified queries), so the
  caller reads the champion default (``research_frame=None`` path) and the run is
  byte-identical. Constructing the projection NEVER raises for a shape problem.

Nothing here is wired ON by default. ``fs_researcher_query_gen`` /
``live_retriever`` / ``run_one_query`` accept a ``retrieval_plan=None`` kwarg
(default None => champion behaviour); the projection is threaded only when the
``PG_GATE`` flag is set and an artifact exists. See :mod:`gate_flags`.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Any, Optional

from src.polaris_graph.planning.planning_gate_schema import (
    FORCE_HARD,
    PlanningGateArtifact,
    QueryIntent,
    ResearchContract,
    ResearchExecutionPlan,
)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# source_type -> evidence_need routing (the "GO FIND journals" map)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Maps a contract ``scope.source_types`` value (free-string, model-emitted) onto
# the field-agnostic ``EVIDENCE_NEEDS`` enum the need-type router routes adapters
# off. This is the load-bearing S2 routing: a "journal articles only" contract
# term (task-72) routes ``primary_literature`` = the S2 + OpenAlex + scholarly-
# graph adapters, so FS goes and FINDS journals from the first query instead of
# filtering a general corpus afterwards.
#
# Each key is matched as a case-insensitive SUBSTRING of the normalized source
# type, so "peer-reviewed journal article", "academic paper", "scholarly
# article" all route primary_literature. Unmatched source types contribute no
# need (the router's safe generic fallback applies), never an error.
_SOURCE_TYPE_NEED_MAP: tuple[tuple[str, str], ...] = (
    ("journal", "primary_literature"),
    ("peer-review", "primary_literature"),
    ("peer review", "primary_literature"),
    ("academic", "primary_literature"),
    ("scholarly", "primary_literature"),
    ("scientific", "primary_literature"),
    ("paper", "primary_literature"),
    ("study", "primary_literature"),
    ("preprint", "primary_literature"),
    ("regulation", "regulatory"),
    ("regulatory", "regulatory"),
    ("guideline", "regulatory"),
    ("law", "legal"),
    ("legal", "legal"),
    ("case law", "legal"),
    ("statute", "legal"),
    ("court", "legal"),
    ("statistic", "statistical"),
    ("census", "statistical"),
    ("survey data", "statistical"),
    ("dataset", "datasets"),
    ("standard", "standards"),
    ("news", "news_press"),
    ("press", "news_press"),
    ("media", "news_press"),
    ("filing", "company_filings"),
    ("sec ", "company_filings"),
    ("10-k", "company_filings"),
    ("annual report", "company_filings"),
)


def _norm(s: Any) -> str:
    return str(s or "").strip().lower()


def source_type_to_need(source_type: Any) -> Optional[str]:
    """Route a single contract ``source_type`` value onto an ``EVIDENCE_NEEDS``
    token, or ``None`` if it matches no known family (the router falls back to
    the safe generic set — never an error)."""
    st = _norm(source_type)
    if not st:
        return None
    for needle, need in _SOURCE_TYPE_NEED_MAP:
        if needle in st:
            return need
    return None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# The projection
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


@dataclass
class RetrievalProjection:
    """Compile-time retrieval view of a pinned contract + plan.

    Built by :func:`from_artifact` (or :meth:`ResearchContract.to_research_frame`
    via the thin methods added to the schema). Holds only plain data; the
    ``research_frame`` is materialized lazily by :meth:`to_research_frame` so this
    dataclass has no heavy import at construction.
    """

    contract: ResearchContract
    plan: ResearchExecutionPlan
    original_prompt: str = ""

    # Precomputed routing tokens (validated against the frame enum lazily).
    evidence_needs: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    jurisdictions: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    entities: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    comparators: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    # Hard scope-term VALUES (verbatim), folded into query text + surfaced for a
    # backend-native filter by a caller that supports one. NEVER a drop here.
    hard_scope_terms: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    # Soft/preference scope-term values — ranking hints only, never a gate.
    soft_scope_terms: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    # Hard source-language codes/names (e.g. "en"); drive native-language lanes.
    hard_languages: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)

    # -- amplified query text (the FS frontier seed) --------------------------

    def to_amplified_queries(self, *, base_question: str = "") -> list[str]:
        """The gate's query TEXT for ``run_live_retrieval(amplified_queries=...)``.

        Built from the plan's MANDATORY query intents (concepts + required
        terms), each scope-anchored by folding the hard scope terms into the
        query string so the FIRST query already carries "journal" / "English"
        etc. Then per-entity sub-queries and native-language lanes are ADDED.
        Order-stable, de-duplicated (case-insensitively), never truncated here.

        This is strictly ADDITIVE: it returns candidate query strings; the caller
        (FS-Researcher / live_retriever) runs each through the UNCHANGED
        per-query retrieval + frozen faithfulness engine. Nothing is dropped.
        """
        out: list[str] = []
        seen: set[str] = set()

        def _add(q: str) -> None:
            qs = (q or "").strip()
            if not qs:
                return
            key = qs.lower()
            if key in seen:
                return
            seen.add(key)
            out.append(qs)

        scope_suffix = " ".join(self.hard_scope_terms).strip()

        for qi in self.plan.mandatory_query_intents():
            for text in _intent_query_texts(qi):
                if scope_suffix and scope_suffix.lower() not in text.lower():
                    _add(f"{text} {scope_suffix}")
                else:
                    _add(text)

        # per-entity sub-queries (sub-entity discovery), anchored on the base
        # question so a bare entity does not drift into its broad field.
        anchor = (base_question or self.original_prompt or "").strip()
        for ent in self.entities:
            ent = (ent or "").strip()
            if not ent:
                continue
            if anchor:
                _add(f"{ent} {anchor}")
            else:
                _add(ent)

        # native-language lanes: for a HARD source-language, ADD the scope-anchored
        # query in that language slot (the routing hint; the multilingual lane in
        # FS translates). We surface a language-tagged variant of the first few
        # mandatory concepts so a "source: English-language" rule reaches the
        # multilingual reserve as an explicit lane rather than being inferred.
        # (English is the pipeline default; a non-English hard language adds a
        # native tag so the reserve fires.)
        for lang in self.hard_languages:
            lang = (lang or "").strip()
            if not lang or lang.lower() in ("en", "english"):
                continue
            for qi in self.plan.mandatory_query_intents()[:2]:
                for text in _intent_query_texts(qi):
                    _add(f"{text} ({lang})")

        return out

    # -- scope terms ----------------------------------------------------------

    def to_scope_terms(self) -> dict[str, list[str]]:
        """The hard/soft scope split + language lanes, as plain lists.

        A caller that supports a backend-native filter reads ``hard`` for the
        explicit prohibitions/requirements (journal-only, English-only) and
        ``soft`` for ranking preferences. This is a compile view — it never
        applies a filter itself. The 997-guard reads the union count.
        """
        return {
            "hard": list(self.hard_scope_terms),
            "soft": list(self.soft_scope_terms),
            "languages": list(self.hard_languages),
            "evidence_needs": list(self.evidence_needs),
        }

    # -- research frame (backend routing) -------------------------------------

    def to_research_frame(self) -> Any:
        """Materialize a champion :class:`ResearchFrame` for backend routing.

        ``evidence_needs`` route the need-type registry (primary_literature ->
        S2/OpenAlex scholarly adapters = GO FIND journals). Entities/comparators
        seed the anchor tokens. Jurisdictions route jurisdiction scopes. Runs the
        frame's own ``validate_evidence_needs`` so a routed need is always a valid
        enum member; an unroutable source type simply contributes no need (safe
        generic fallback), never an error.

        Returns ``None`` when the projection carries NOTHING to route (empty
        needs AND no entities/jurisdictions) so the caller keeps the champion
        ``research_frame=None`` (byte-identical) path.
        """
        if not (self.evidence_needs or self.entities or self.jurisdictions
                or self.comparators):
            return None
        from src.polaris_graph.planning.research_planner import (  # noqa: PLC0415
            ResearchFrame,
            validate_evidence_needs,
            validate_jurisdiction_shapes,
        )
        # validate/normalize; drop any that fail SHAPE rather than raise (compile
        # view is fail-open — a bad token contributes nothing, never aborts).
        try:
            needs = validate_evidence_needs(self.evidence_needs)
        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
            needs = [n for n in self.evidence_needs
                     if _safe_need(n)]
        try:
            juris = validate_jurisdiction_shapes(self.jurisdictions)
        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
            juris = []
        return ResearchFrame(
            entities=list(self.entities),
            comparators=list(self.comparators),
            constraints=list(self.hard_scope_terms),
            evidence_needs=needs,
            jurisdictions=juris,
        )

    def to_protocol(self, *, base_question: str = "") -> Optional[dict[str, Any]]:
        """The anchor protocol for ``validate_amplified_queries`` (from the frame).

        ``None`` when there is no frame to anchor (keeps the champion path)."""
        frame = self.to_research_frame()
        if frame is None:
            return None
        anchor = (base_question or self.original_prompt or "").strip()
        return frame.to_anchor_protocol(anchor)

    # -- the mechanized 997-guard (planning level) ----------------------------

    def candidate_query_count(self, *, base_question: str = "") -> int:
        """Count the candidate query/evidence lanes this projection ADDS.

        The 997-guard: a caller asserts ``gate_count >= no_gate_count`` so the
        gate path can NEVER reduce the candidate query/evidence-need count vs the
        no-gate path — at PLANNING level, before any fetch. Counts distinct
        amplified queries plus routed evidence needs (each a discovery lane)."""
        return (
            len(self.to_amplified_queries(base_question=base_question))
            + len(self.evidence_needs)
        )


def _safe_need(n: Any) -> bool:
    from src.polaris_graph.planning.research_planner import EVIDENCE_NEEDS  # noqa: PLC0415
    return _norm(n) in EVIDENCE_NEEDS


def _intent_query_texts(qi: QueryIntent) -> list[str]:
    """The query string(s) a single :class:`QueryIntent` contributes.

    Prefers a concept+required-term join; falls back to alternate phrasings, then
    the raw thread question via concepts. Always returns at least one non-empty
    string when the intent carries any text."""
    parts: list[str] = []
    core = " ".join([*qi.concepts, *qi.required_terms]).strip()
    if core:
        parts.append(core)
    for alt in qi.alternate_phrasings:
        alt = (alt or "").strip()
        if alt:
            parts.append(alt)
    if not parts:
        # nothing structured — fall back to any single concept/phrasing.
        for cand in [*qi.concepts, *qi.alternate_phrasings]:
            if (cand or "").strip():
                parts.append(cand.strip())
                break
    return parts


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Compilation from a pinned artifact / contract
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Scope dimensions whose VALUES are folded into query text as scope terms.
_SCOPE_TEXT_DIMENSIONS: tuple[str, ...] = (
    "scope.source_types",
    "scope.source_quality",  # high-quality/peer-reviewed steers the citable menu
    "scope.domains",
    "scope.geographies",
    "scope.prohibited",  # a prohibition is disclosed as a term the caller may filter
)
_LANGUAGE_DIMENSIONS: tuple[str, ...] = (
    "scope.source_languages",
    "scope.languages",
)


def from_contract_and_plan(
    contract: ResearchContract,
    plan: ResearchExecutionPlan,
    *,
    original_prompt: str = "",
) -> RetrievalProjection:
    """Compile a :class:`RetrievalProjection` from a contract + plan.

    Pure + deterministic. Walks the contract's scope + content terms and the
    plan's query intents to derive routing tokens. Fail-open: a degraded/empty
    contract yields an empty projection (the caller keeps the champion path).
    """
    evidence_needs: list[str] = []
    jurisdictions: list[str] = []
    entities: list[str] = []
    comparators: list[str] = []
    hard_scope_terms: list[str] = []
    soft_scope_terms: list[str] = []
    hard_languages: list[str] = []

    def _dedup_add(lst: list[str], val: Any) -> None:
        s = str(val or "").strip()
        if not s:
            return
        if s.lower() not in {x.lower() for x in lst}:
            lst.append(s)

    for term in contract.scope:
        dim = _norm(term.dimension)
        val = term.value
        if val in (None, "", [], {}):
            continue
        vals = val if isinstance(val, (list, tuple)) else [val]

        if dim in _LANGUAGE_DIMENSIONS:
            if term.force == FORCE_HARD:
                for v in vals:
                    _dedup_add(hard_languages, v)
            continue

        # source_types route evidence needs (the GO-FIND-journals map).
        if dim == "scope.source_types":
            for v in vals:
                need = source_type_to_need(v)
                if need:
                    _dedup_add(evidence_needs, need)

        if dim in ("scope.jurisdictions", "scope.geographies"):
            for v in vals:
                _dedup_add(jurisdictions, str(v).strip().upper()[:4])

        # fold the value into the hard/soft scope text bucket.
        bucket = hard_scope_terms if term.force == FORCE_HARD else soft_scope_terms
        if dim in _SCOPE_TEXT_DIMENSIONS or term.force == FORCE_HARD:
            for v in vals:
                _dedup_add(bucket, v)

    # entities + comparators from the content spec (sub-entity discovery breadth).
    for ent in contract.content_terms:
        if _norm(ent.dimension).startswith("content.entit") and ent.value:
            _dedup_add(entities, ent.value)
    for cov in contract.coverage:
        if cov.kind in ("entity",) and cov.statement.value:
            _dedup_add(entities, cov.statement.value)
        if cov.kind in ("comparison",):
            for c in cov.compare_on:
                if c.value:
                    _dedup_add(comparators, c.value)

    return RetrievalProjection(
        contract=contract,
        plan=plan,
        original_prompt=original_prompt,
        evidence_needs=evidence_needs,
        jurisdictions=jurisdictions,
        entities=entities,
        comparators=comparators,
        hard_scope_terms=hard_scope_terms,
        soft_scope_terms=soft_scope_terms,
        hard_languages=hard_languages,
    )


def from_artifact(artifact: PlanningGateArtifact) -> RetrievalProjection:
    """Compile a :class:`RetrievalProjection` from a pinned artifact."""
    return from_contract_and_plan(
        artifact.contract,
        artifact.plan,
        original_prompt=artifact.original_prompt,
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Transitional adapter: champion ResearchPlan -> projection (the FS seam fix)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


class _ChampionPlanProjection(RetrievalProjection):
    """A projection whose amplified queries ARE the champion plan's ``sub_queries``.

    S2 seam fix (design §5): the FS-Researcher branch in ``run_one_query``
    currently passes only ``_clean_question`` and DISCARDS
    ``_research_plan.sub_queries``. This adapter wraps a champion
    ``research_planner.ResearchPlan`` as a :class:`RetrievalProjection` so the FS
    frontier is seeded with those sub-queries (the gate's pre-retrieval lanes)
    plus the frame's ``evidence_needs`` routing — BEFORE any fetch. Until the full
    gate artifact is wired into ``run_one_query`` (a later step), this carries the
    already-computed champion plan into the FS query frontier instead of throwing
    it away. It NEVER drops a source; it only ADDS the sub-queries the FS branch
    was discarding, and only when ``PG_GATE`` is ON.
    """

    def __init__(self, research_plan: Any, *, original_prompt: str = "") -> None:
        frame = getattr(research_plan, "frame", None)
        sub_queries = list(getattr(research_plan, "sub_queries", []) or [])
        super().__init__(
            contract=ResearchContract(),
            plan=ResearchExecutionPlan(),
            original_prompt=original_prompt,
            evidence_needs=list(getattr(frame, "evidence_needs", []) or []),
            jurisdictions=list(getattr(frame, "jurisdictions", []) or []),
            entities=list(getattr(frame, "entities", []) or []),
            comparators=list(getattr(frame, "comparators", []) or []),
        )
        self._sub_queries = sub_queries
        self._frame = frame

    def to_amplified_queries(self, *, base_question: str = "") -> list[str]:
        out: list[str] = []
        seen: set[str] = set()
        for q in self._sub_queries:
            qs = (q or "").strip()
            if qs and qs.lower() not in seen:
                seen.add(qs.lower())
                out.append(qs)
        return out

    def to_research_frame(self) -> Any:
        return self._frame

    def to_protocol(self, *, base_question: str = "") -> Optional[dict[str, Any]]:
        if self._frame is None:
            return None
        try:
            return self._frame.to_anchor_protocol(
                (base_question or self.original_prompt or "").strip()
            )
        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
            return None


def from_champion_plan(
    research_plan: Any, *, original_prompt: str = ""
) -> Optional[RetrievalProjection]:
    """Adapt a champion ``ResearchPlan`` into a projection, or ``None`` if it has
    no usable sub-queries (keep the champion FS path)."""
    if research_plan is None:
        return None
    if not list(getattr(research_plan, "sub_queries", []) or []):
        return None
    return _ChampionPlanProjection(research_plan, original_prompt=original_prompt)

1	"""I-scope-001 — the generic SCOPE + TIMELINE enforcement plan builder.
2	
3	Generalizes the PROVEN date-window WEIGHT seam (``evidence_selector`` ~2881: build a per-URL
4	demote map + an out-of-window tail-partition set + disclosed exclusion records) to arbitrary
5	SCOPE facets driven by the ontology. It emits, for one corpus + one protocol:
6	
7	  * ``url_to_scope_weight``   — multiplicative (0,1] demote for WEIGHT-demoted sources
8	                               (mirrors ``url_to_date_weight``); folded into the SAME
9	                               selection sort key. WEIGHT path — never a drop.
10	  * ``out_of_scope_urls``     — the tail-partition set (mirrors ``_oow_urls``).
11	  * ``grounding_excluded_ids``— HARD-mode urls masked OUT of the billed grounding set
12	                               ``evidence_for_gen`` (restrict-to / exclude-hard / hard
13	                               timeline). KEPT in the pool + disclosure, never deleted.
14	  * ``must_include_urls``     — op='include' boost/pin flag (additive, non-demoting).
15	  * ``scope_excluded_records``— PRISMA-style telemetry, one row per decision.
16	
17	DNA §-1.3: WEIGHT-DON'T-FILTER + DISCLOSE. A HARD mask removes a source from the ANSWER
18	GROUNDING surface only, never from the evidence pool / strict_verify / NLI / D8 / provenance
19	(the ONE hard gate stays byte-untouched). Fail-open: an unresolved facet => neutral (weight
20	1.0, never punished). Default-OFF ``PG_SCOPE_CONSTRAINT_ENFORCE`` => empty plan => byte-
21	identical selection + no grounding mask. All knobs config-driven (LAW VI).
22	"""
23	from __future__ import annotations
24	
25	import logging
26	import os
27	import re
28	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
29	from typing import Any, Mapping
30	
31	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.scope_facet_classifier import (
32	    classify_source_facets,
33	    facet_default_weight,
34	    load_scope_ontology,
35	)
36	
37	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.constraint_enforcement")
38	
39	_ENFORCE_FLAG = "PG_SCOPE_CONSTRAINT_ENFORCE"
40	_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"0", "false", "no", "off", "disabled", ""})
41	
42	_YEAR_RE = re.compile(r"\b(19|20|21)\d{2}\b")
43	_ISO_YM_RE = re.compile(r"\b((?:19|20|21)\d{2})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])\b")
44	
45	
46	def scope_enforcement_enabled() -> bool:
47	    """I-scope-001 enforcement kill-switch. DEFAULT OFF (operator activates on the slate).
48	    OFF => ``build_scope_enforcement`` returns an EMPTY plan => byte-identical selection."""
49	    return os.getenv(_ENFORCE_FLAG, "0").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
50	
51	
52	@dataclass
53	class ScopeEnforcementPlan:
54	    """The demote / mask / boost decisions for one corpus under one protocol's scope+timeline
55	    intent. Empty plan => byte-identical widest+deepest run."""
56	
57	    url_to_scope_weight: dict[str, float] = field(default_factory=dict)
58	    out_of_scope_urls: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)
59	    grounding_excluded_ids: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)
60	    must_include_urls: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)
61	    scope_excluded_records: list[dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
62	    scope_disclosed_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
63	
64	    def is_empty(self) -> bool:
65	        return not (
66	            self.url_to_scope_weight
67	            or self.out_of_scope_urls
68	            or self.grounding_excluded_ids
69	            or self.must_include_urls
70	        )
71	
72	
73	def _field(source: "Any", *names: str) -> "Any":
74	    if isinstance(source, Mapping):
75	        for n in names:
76	            if n in source and source.get(n) is not None:
77	                return source.get(n)
78	        return None
79	    for n in names:
80	        v = getattr(source, n, None)
81	        if v is not None:
82	            return v
83	    return None
84	
85	
86	def _row_url(row: "Any") -> str:
87	    return str(_field(row, "source_url", "url") or "")
88	
89	
90	def _row_pub_ym(row: "Any") -> "tuple[int, int | None] | None":
91	    """Best-effort (year, month|None) from a row's date fields. None when undated
92	    (fail-open — an undated row is NEVER excluded by a timeline window).
93	
94	    Month-precision keys (``pub_date`` is the production month field emitted by
95	    live_retriever) are read BEFORE the year-only fallbacks (``publication_year`` /
96	    ``year``) so month precision wins: a live row carrying ``pub_date``="2023-05"
97	    plus a year-only ``publication_year``=2023 resolves to (2023, 5), not (2023, None).
98	    Reading only the year would degrade every real row to YEAR precision and wrongly
99	    hard-mask an in-window month source under a HARD month-precision cutoff (§-1.3)."""
100	    for key in ("pub_date", "publication_date", "published", "date", "publication_year", "year"):
101	        v = _field(row, key)
102	        if v is None:
103	            continue
104	        s = str(v)
105	        m = _ISO_YM_RE.search(s)
106	        if m:
107	            return (int(m.group(1)), int(m.group(2)))
108	        y = _YEAR_RE.search(s)
109	        if y:
110	            return (int(y.group(0)), None)
111	    return None
112	
113	
114	def _parse_window_bound(iso: "str | None") -> "tuple[int, int | None] | None":
115	    if not iso:
116	        return None
117	    s = str(iso)
118	    m = _ISO_YM_RE.search(s)
119	    if m:
120	        return (int(m.group(1)), int(m.group(2)))
121	    y = _YEAR_RE.search(s)
122	    if y:
123	        return (int(y.group(0)), None)
124	    return None
125	
126	
127	def _ym_index(ym: "tuple[int, int | None]", *, ceil: bool) -> int:
128	    """Month index (year*12+month). A year-only bound uses month 1 for a floor and 12 for a
129	    ceiling so a whole-year window is inclusive."""
130	    year, month = ym
131	    if month is None:
132	        month = 12 if ceil else 1
133	    return year * 12 + month
134	
135	
136	def _row_out_of_window(
137	    row: "Any", start_idx: "int | None", end_idx: "int | None"
138	) -> bool:
139	    """True iff the row has an EXPLICIT publication date outside [start, end]. Undated =>
140	    False (fail-open, kept)."""
141	    pub = _row_pub_ym(row)
142	    if pub is None:
143	        return False
144	    idx = _ym_index((pub[0], pub[1]), ceil=False)
145	    if start_idx is not None and idx < start_idx:
146	        return True
147	    if end_idx is not None:
148	        # compare at the row's own precision against the ceiling
149	        row_ceil = _ym_index((pub[0], pub[1]), ceil=(pub[1] is None))
150	        if row_ceil > end_idx:
151	            return True
152	    return False
153	
154	
155	def _facet_ids(facets: list[dict[str, Any]], op: str, strictness: "str | None") -> list[str]:
156	    out = []
157	    for f in facets:
158	        if not isinstance(f, dict):
159	            continue
160	        if str(f.get("op")) != op:
161	            continue
162	        if strictness is not None and str(f.get("strictness")) != strictness:
163	            continue
164	        fid = str(f.get("facet_id") or "")
165	        if fid:
166	            out.append(fid)
167	    return out
168	
169	
170	def _named_match(label: str, identity: dict[str, Any], row: "Any") -> bool:
171	    """Match a named-include source to a row by its org acronym / label tokens against the
172	    row url / host / title. Deterministic, conservative (acronym is case-sensitive)."""
173	    hay = " ".join(
174	        str(_field(row, k) or "") for k in ("source_url", "url", "title")
175	    )
176	    acronym = str(identity.get("acronym") or "").strip()
177	    if acronym and re.search(r"\b" + re.escape(acronym) + r"\b", hay):
178	        return True
179	    # fall back to a distinctive lowercase token from the label (>= 4 chars)
180	    for tok in re.findall(r"[A-Za-z]{4,}", label):
181	        if tok.lower() in ("guidelines", "guideline", "reports", "report", "data",
182	                           "publications", "publication", "standards", "framework"):
183	            continue
184	        if tok.lower() in hay.lower():
185	            return True
186	    return False
187	
188	
189	def build_scope_enforcement(
190	    protocol: "dict[str, Any] | None",
191	    evidence_rows: "list[Any] | None",
192	    ontology: "dict[str, Any] | None" = None,
193	) -> ScopeEnforcementPlan:
194	    """Build the scope+timeline enforcement plan for one corpus (§2.2).
195	
196	    Reads ``protocol['scope_constraints']`` (from ``ScopeConstraints.to_dict``) + the timeline
197	    strictness inside ``protocol['user_constraints']`` + the date window from
198	    ``protocol['date_range']``. Classifies each row via ``classify_source_facets`` and applies
199	    the per-(op,strictness) rule. Returns an EMPTY plan when the enforce flag is OFF or when no
200	    scope/timeline constraint was stated (byte-identical). Pure; never raises."""
201	    plan = ScopeEnforcementPlan()
202	    if not scope_enforcement_enabled():
203	        return plan
204	    if not protocol:
205	        return plan
206	    rows = list(evidence_rows or [])
207	    if not rows:
208	        return plan
209	
210	    ont = ontology if ontology is not None else load_scope_ontology()
211	    sc = protocol.get("scope_constraints") or {}
212	    uc = protocol.get("user_constraints") or {}
213	    facets = list(sc.get("facets") or []) if isinstance(sc, dict) else []
214	    named_include = list(sc.get("named_include") or []) if isinstance(sc, dict) else []
215	
216	    prefer_weight = _facet_ids(facets, "prefer", "weight")
217	    prefer_hard = _facet_ids(facets, "prefer", "hard")
218	    include_ids = _facet_ids(facets, "include", None)
219	    exclude_weight = _facet_ids(facets, "exclude", "weight")
220	    exclude_hard = _facet_ids(facets, "exclude", "hard")
221	
222	    # trigger spans for disclosure
223	    span_by_facet = {
224	        str(f.get("facet_id")): str(f.get("trigger_span") or "")
225	        for f in facets if isinstance(f, dict)
226	    }
227	
228	    # timeline (hard) window
229	    timeline_strictness = str(uc.get("timeline_strictness") or "weight")
230	    dr = protocol.get("date_range") or {}
231	    if isinstance(dr, (list, tuple)):
232	        _s_iso, _e_iso = (dr[0] if len(dr) > 0 else None), (dr[1] if len(dr) > 1 else None)
233	    elif isinstance(dr, dict):
234	        _s_iso, _e_iso = dr.get("start"), dr.get("end")
235	    else:
236	        _s_iso, _e_iso = None, None
237	    _start = _parse_window_bound(_s_iso)
238	    _end = _parse_window_bound(_e_iso)
239	    _start_idx = _ym_index(_start, ceil=False) if _start else None
240	    _end_idx = _ym_index(_end, ceil=(_end[1] is None)) if _end else None
241	    timeline_hard_active = (
242	        timeline_strictness == "hard" and (_start_idx is not None or _end_idx is not None)
243	    )
244	
245	    have_scope = bool(
246	        prefer_weight or prefer_hard or include_ids or exclude_weight or exclude_hard
247	        or named_include
248	    )
249	    if not have_scope and not timeline_hard_active:
250	        return plan
251	
252	    def _min_weight(ids: list[str]) -> float:
253	        ws = [facet_default_weight(i, ont) for i in ids]
254	        return min(ws) if ws else facet_default_weight("", ont)
255	
256	    for row in rows:
257	        url = _row_url(row)
258	        if not url:
259	            continue
260	        try:
261	            row_facets, _basis = classify_source_facets(row, ont)
262	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - fail-open: an unresolved row is neutral
263	            row_facets = set()
264	
265	        # op='include' — additive boost; non-matching NOT demoted.
266	        matched_include = [i for i in include_ids if i in row_facets]
267	        for named in named_include:
268	            if isinstance(named, dict) and _named_match(
269	                str(named.get("label") or ""), dict(named.get("identity") or {}), row
270	            ):
271	                plan.must_include_urls.add(url)
272	                plan.scope_excluded_records.append({
273	                    "source_url": url, "matched_facet": str(named.get("label") or ""),
274	                    "op": "include", "action": "named_include_boost", "weight": 1.0,
275	                    "reason": "named-include pinned", "trigger_span": str(named.get("label") or ""),
276	                })
277	        if matched_include:
278	            plan.must_include_urls.add(url)
279	            plan.scope_excluded_records.append({
280	                "source_url": url, "matched_facet": ",".join(matched_include),
281	                "op": "include", "action": "include_boost", "weight": 1.0,
282	                "reason": "in requested include facet",
283	                "trigger_span": span_by_facet.get(matched_include[0], ""),
284	            })
285	
286	        # HARD exclusion — masked from grounding, KEPT in pool + disclosure.
287	        hard_excluded = False
288	        matched_exclude_hard = [i for i in exclude_hard if i in row_facets]
289	        if matched_exclude_hard:
290	            hard_excluded = True
291	            plan.grounding_excluded_ids.add(url)
292	            plan.scope_excluded_records.append({
293	                "source_url": url, "matched_facet": ",".join(matched_exclude_hard),
294	                "op": "exclude", "action": "hard_excluded_from_grounding_disclosed",
295	                "weight": 0.0, "reason": "in do-not-use facet (hard)",
296	                "trigger_span": span_by_facet.get(matched_exclude_hard[0], ""),
297	            })
298	        elif prefer_hard and not any(i in row_facets for i in prefer_hard):
299	            hard_excluded = True
300	            plan.grounding_excluded_ids.add(url)
301	            plan.scope_excluded_records.append({
302	                "source_url": url, "matched_facet": ",".join(prefer_hard),
303	                "op": "prefer", "action": "hard_excluded_from_grounding_disclosed",
304	                "weight": 0.0, "reason": "outside restrict-to facet (hard)",
305	                "trigger_span": span_by_facet.get(prefer_hard[0], ""),
306	            })
307	
308	        # HARD timeline — out-of-window masked from grounding, KEPT + disclosed.
309	        if timeline_hard_active and _row_out_of_window(row, _start_idx, _end_idx):
310	            hard_excluded = True
311	            plan.grounding_excluded_ids.add(url)
312	            _pub = _row_pub_ym(row)
313	            plan.scope_excluded_records.append({
314	                "source_url": url,
315	                "matched_facet": "timeline",
316	                "op": "timeline", "action": "timeline_hard_excluded_disclosed",
317	                "weight": 0.0,
318	                "reason": "outside hard timeline window",
319	                "pub_ym": (f"{_pub[0]:04d}-{_pub[1]:02d}" if _pub and _pub[1]
320	                           else (str(_pub[0]) if _pub else None)),
321	                "trigger_span": str(uc.get("timeline_trigger_span") or ""),
322	            })
323	
324	        if hard_excluded:
325	            plan.scope_disclosed_rows.append({
326	                "source_url": url,
327	                "disclosed": "hard_excluded_from_grounding_kept_in_corpus",
328	            })
329	            continue
330	
331	        # WEIGHT demote (prefer-weight miss OR exclude-weight hit). Multiplicative, kept.
332	        w = 1.0
333	        demote_reason = ""
334	        demote_facet = ""
335	        if prefer_weight and not any(i in row_facets for i in prefer_weight):
336	            w = min(w, _min_weight(prefer_weight))
337	            demote_reason = "outside preferred facet"
338	            demote_facet = ",".join(prefer_weight)
339	        matched_exclude_weight = [i for i in exclude_weight if i in row_facets]
340	        if matched_exclude_weight:
341	            w = min(w, _min_weight(matched_exclude_weight))
342	            demote_reason = "in de-emphasized facet"
343	            demote_facet = ",".join(matched_exclude_weight)
344	        # P1 fix (§2.2/§2.4 include != prefer): a source the user explicitly INCLUDED
345	        # (op='include' / named-include) is NEVER also demoted — include is an ADDITIVE
346	        # boost, so it must not sink its own pinned source. The include block above ran
347	        # first this iteration, so ``must_include_urls`` already carries this url when it
348	        # was included. Skipping the demote keeps the disclosure honest (a source is never
349	        # recorded as both boosted and demoted) and matches the selector's include-pin.
350	        if 0.0 < w < 1.0 and url not in plan.must_include_urls:
351	            plan.url_to_scope_weight[url] = w
352	            plan.out_of_scope_urls.add(url)
353	            plan.scope_excluded_records.append({
354	                "source_url": url, "matched_facet": demote_facet,
355	                "op": ("exclude" if matched_exclude_weight else "prefer"),
356	                "action": "demoted_out_of_scope_kept", "weight": w,
357	                "reason": demote_reason,
358	                "trigger_span": span_by_facet.get(
359	                    (demote_facet.split(",")[0] if demote_facet else ""), ""
360	                ),
361	            })
362	
363	    if not plan.is_empty():
364	        logger.info(
365	            "[scope_enforce] I-scope-001: demoted=%d hard_masked=%d boosted=%d "
366	            "(timeline_hard=%s)",
367	            len(plan.url_to_scope_weight), len(plan.grounding_excluded_ids),
368	            len(plan.must_include_urls), timeline_hard_active,
369	        )
370	    return plan
371

121	    "chinese": "zh", "japanese": "ja", "portuguese": "pt", "italian": "it",

131	    """B10 kill-switch. DEFAULT OFF (a NEW intake behavior; the operator activates

133	    return os.getenv(_ENV_FLAG, "0").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES

162	            and self.date_end_year is None
163	            and self.language is None
164	            and not self.journal_only

172	        mo = self.date_start_month or 1
173	        return f"{self.date_start_year:04d}-{mo:02d}-01"
174	
175	    def date_end_iso(self) -> Optional[str]:
176	        """ISO ceiling bound for protocol.date_range. When a month is known the
177	        bound carries it ('YYYY-MM' — the selector compares at month precision);
178	        else 'YYYY-12-31' (whole-year ceiling)."""
179	        if self.date_end_year is None:
180	            return None
181	        if self.date_end_month:
182	            return f"{self.date_end_year:04d}-{self.date_end_month:02d}"
183	        return f"{self.date_end_year:04d}-12-31"
184	
185	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
186	        return {
187	            "date_start_year": self.date_start_year,
188	            "date_end_year": self.date_end_year,
189	            "date_start_month": self.date_start_month,
190	            "date_end_month": self.date_end_month,
191	            "date_start_iso": self.date_start_iso(),
192	            "date_end_iso": self.date_end_iso(),
193	            "language": self.language,
194	            "journal_only_dormant": self.journal_only,
195	            "timeline_strictness": self.timeline_strictness,
196	            "timeline_trigger_span": self.timeline_trigger_span,
197	            "raw_directives": list(self.raw_directives),
198	            "source": self.source,
199	        }

"""I-deepfix-001 B10 (2026-06-28) — user hard-constraint intake extractor.

A research prompt may carry HARD user constraints the pipeline must honor:
  * a date window ("studies since 2020", "before 2018", "in the last 5 years"),
  * a language ("English-language sources only"),
  * a source-type directive ("peer-reviewed journals only" — journal-only stays
    DORMANT per operator veto; we extract+disclose it but do NOT enforce a drop).

Today these are dropped at intake, so recency_tiebreak can fight a max-date and
out-of-window sources are cited. This module EXTRACTS a structured
``UserConstraints`` block so the downstream layers can:
  - apply the date window server-side at OpenAlex (to_publication_date) and as a
    DISCLOSED soft floor at selection (out-of-window rows demoted + recorded,
    never silently dropped per §-1.3),
  - invert/disable recency_tiebreak when a max-date is present,
  - demote non-target-language rows as a disclosed weight,
  - render an adherence-disclosure banner (DRB-II instruction-following credit).

EXTRACTION: dateparser (BSD-3, in-tree) + deterministic regex primary; an
injected ``llm_fn(prompt) -> json_str`` GLM-5.2 fallback for prose the regex
misses. Pure + offline-testable: the LLM is injected, never imported here.
Faithfulness engine UNTOUCHED — this is intake metadata only. All knobs env
(LAW VI). Journal-only is EXTRACTED but flagged dormant (operator veto).
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import json
import logging
import os
import re
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Any, Callable, Optional

logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.intake_constraint_extractor")

_ENV_FLAG = "PG_EXTRACT_USER_CONSTRAINTS"
_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"0", "false", "no", "off", "disabled", ""})

# Year sanity window — a 4-digit number outside this range is not a publication year.
_MIN_YEAR = 1900
_MAX_YEAR = 2100

_YEAR_RE = re.compile(r"\b(19|20|21)\d{2}\b")
# "since/after/from YYYY" => start floor; "before/until/by/up to YYYY" => end ceiling.
_SINCE_RE = re.compile(r"\b(?:since|after|from|starting(?:\s+in)?|>=|>)\s+((?:19|20|21)\d{2})\b", re.I)
_BEFORE_RE = re.compile(r"\b(?:before|until|by|up\s+to|prior\s+to|earlier\s+than|<=|<)\s+((?:19|20|21)\d{2})\b", re.I)

# I-deepfix-001 Codex wave-2 P1: MONTH-precision bounds. "before June 2023" /
# "since March 2020" / "2023-06" must be captured at MONTH granularity so the
# selector can enforce a sub-year ceiling (a year-only bound let post-June-2023
# rows survive a "before June 2023" window). The bare-year regexes above do NOT
# match "before June 2023" (the month sits between the keyword and the year), so
# these month matchers are additive and never conflict with the year ones.
_MON_NAMES = {
    "january": 1, "february": 2, "march": 3, "april": 4, "may": 5, "june": 6,
    "july": 7, "august": 8, "september": 9, "october": 10, "november": 11,
    "december": 12, "jan": 1, "feb": 2, "mar": 3, "apr": 4, "jun": 6, "jul": 7,
    "aug": 8, "sep": 9, "sept": 9, "oct": 10, "nov": 11, "dec": 12,
}
_MON_RE = (
    r"(jan(?:uary)?|feb(?:ruary)?|mar(?:ch)?|apr(?:il)?|may|jun(?:e)?|jul(?:y)?|"
    r"aug(?:ust)?|sep(?:t|tember)?|oct(?:ober)?|nov(?:ember)?|dec(?:ember)?)"
)
_SINCE_MONTH_RE = re.compile(
    r"\b(?:since|after|from|starting(?:\s+in)?)\s+" + _MON_RE
    + r"\s+((?:19|20|21)\d{2})\b", re.I)
_BEFORE_MONTH_RE = re.compile(
    r"\b(?:before|until|by|up\s+to|prior\s+to|earlier\s+than)\s+" + _MON_RE
    + r"\s+((?:19|20|21)\d{2})\b", re.I)
# ISO "YYYY-MM" is bound to its DIRECTION (Codex wave-2 P1: a blind ISO matcher
# inverted "since 2020-03" into an END ceiling). A bare floating "YYYY-MM" with no
# before/since keyword is intentionally NOT treated as a bound.
_SINCE_ISO_RE = re.compile(
    r"\b(?:since|after|from|starting(?:\s+in)?)\s+"
    r"((?:19|20|21)\d{2})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])\b", re.I)
_BEFORE_ISO_RE = re.compile(
    r"\b(?:before|until|by|up\s+to|prior\s+to|earlier\s+than|through)\s+"
    r"((?:19|20|21)\d{2})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])\b", re.I)
# "in the last N years" relative window.
_LAST_N_RE = re.compile(r"\b(?:in\s+the\s+)?(?:last|past|recent)\s+(\d{1,2})\s+years?\b", re.I)
# I-scope-001 [FIX-3]: HARD timeline exclusivity/prohibition clause. Fires ONLY on an
# explicit exclusivity token ("strictly"/"only") or a prohibition ("no sources after",
# "must predate") ADJACENT to a date clause — never on a plain "before June 2023" (which
# stays 'weight'). Captures the verbatim trigger span. Ambiguity => weight (HARD is opt-in).
_HARD_TIMELINE_RE = re.compile(
    r"\b(?:"
    r"(?:strictly|only)\s+(?:before|after|prior\s+to|until|up\s+to|since)\s+"
    r"(?:" + _MON_RE + r"\s+)?(?:19|20|21)\d{2}"
    r"|no\s+(?:sources?|articles?|papers?|studies?|publications?|literature)\s+"
    r"(?:after|before|since|from|newer\s+than|older\s+than)\s+"
    r"(?:" + _MON_RE + r"\s+)?(?:19|20|21)\d{2}"
    r"|must\s+(?:predate|postdate|not\s+(?:cite|use|include)\s+(?:sources?|anything))\s+"
    r"(?:" + _MON_RE + r"\s+)?(?:19|20|21)\d{2}"
    r")\b",
    re.I,
)
# I-scope-001 (drb_72 two-sided intelligence): a HARD "must be based on … before <date>"
# REQUIREMENT clause. This is an EXPLICIT restrict-to directive ("The report NEEDS TO BE
# BASED ON academic research published before June 2023"), not the ambiguity default — so
# it is §-1.3-opt-in HARD (the out-of-window source is grounding-masked but KEPT in the
# pool + disclosure). It fires ONLY when a strong requirement verb (must / need(s) to /
# have to / has to) governs a "based/drawn/grounded on … before <date>" clause within one
# sentence ([^.?!] never crosses a sentence). A plain "before June 2023" (or "studies since
# 2020, before June 2023") carries NO requirement verb, so it stays 'weight' (test_10/16).
_HARD_TIMELINE_REQUIREMENT_RE = re.compile(
    r"\b(?:must|need(?:s|ed)?(?:\s+to)?|have\s+to|has\s+to)\s+(?:be\s+)?"
    r"(?:based|drawn?|grounded|rel(?:y|ied|ying)|restricted|limited)\s+(?:on|upon|from|to)\b"
    r"[^.?!]*?"
    r"\b(?:before|prior\s+to|published\s+before|up\s+to|until|no\s+later\s+than|earlier\s+than)\s+"
    r"(?:" + _MON_RE + r"\s+)?(?:19|20|21)\d{2}",
    re.I,
)
# language directive.
_LANG_RE = re.compile(
    r"\b(english|french|spanish|german|chinese|japanese|portuguese|italian)"
    r"[\-\s]*(?:language)?\s*(?:sources?|articles?|papers?|studies?|only|)\b",
    re.I,
)
_LANG_CODE = {
    "english": "en", "french": "fr", "spanish": "es", "german": "de",
    "chinese": "zh", "japanese": "ja", "portuguese": "pt", "italian": "it",
}
# source-type / journal-only directive (extracted, DORMANT per operator veto).
_JOURNAL_ONLY_RE = re.compile(
    r"\b(?:peer[\-\s]*reviewed|journal[\-\s]*only|published\s+journals?|"
    r"only\s+(?:journals?|peer[\-\s]*reviewed))\b", re.I
)


def extract_user_constraints_enabled() -> bool:
    """B10 kill-switch. DEFAULT OFF (a NEW intake behavior; the operator activates
    it on the slate). Set ``PG_EXTRACT_USER_CONSTRAINTS=1`` to activate."""
    return os.getenv(_ENV_FLAG, "0").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES


@dataclass
class UserConstraints:
    """Structured hard-constraint block extracted from the research prompt.

    All fields optional; an all-empty instance means no constraints detected.
    """

    date_start_year: Optional[int] = None     # inclusive lower bound (publication year)
    date_end_year: Optional[int] = None       # inclusive upper bound
    date_start_month: Optional[int] = None    # 1..12, MONTH precision for the floor (Codex wave-2 P1)
    date_end_month: Optional[int] = None      # 1..12, MONTH precision for the ceiling
    language: Optional[str] = None            # ISO code, e.g. 'en'
    journal_only: bool = False               # EXTRACTED but DORMANT (operator veto)
    # I-scope-001 [FIX-3]: timeline STRICTNESS. Default 'weight' (the existing demote-and-keep
    # path). 'hard' is opt-in — fired ONLY by an explicit exclusivity/prohibition token
    # adjacent to the date clause ("strictly before", "no sources after", "must predate"),
    # so an out-of-window row is masked from the grounding surface (still KEPT in the pool +
    # disclosure). The trigger span is the verbatim phrase that fired it.
    timeline_strictness: str = "weight"      # 'weight' (default demote) | 'hard' (grounding-exclude)
    timeline_trigger_span: str = ""          # the verbatim phrase, e.g. "strictly before June 2023"
    raw_directives: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    source: str = "regex"                    # 'regex' | 'llm' | 'merged'

    def is_empty(self) -> bool:
        return (
            self.date_start_year is None
            and self.date_end_year is None
            and self.language is None
            and not self.journal_only
        )

    def date_start_iso(self) -> Optional[str]:
        """ISO floor bound for protocol.date_range: 'YYYY-MM-01' when a month is
        known, else 'YYYY-01-01', else None."""
        if self.date_start_year is None:
            return None
        mo = self.date_start_month or 1
        return f"{self.date_start_year:04d}-{mo:02d}-01"

    def date_end_iso(self) -> Optional[str]:
        """ISO ceiling bound for protocol.date_range. When a month is known the
        bound carries it ('YYYY-MM' — the selector compares at month precision);
        else 'YYYY-12-31' (whole-year ceiling)."""
        if self.date_end_year is None:
            return None
        if self.date_end_month:
            return f"{self.date_end_year:04d}-{self.date_end_month:02d}"
        return f"{self.date_end_year:04d}-12-31"

    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "date_start_year": self.date_start_year,
            "date_end_year": self.date_end_year,
            "date_start_month": self.date_start_month,
            "date_end_month": self.date_end_month,
            "date_start_iso": self.date_start_iso(),
            "date_end_iso": self.date_end_iso(),
            "language": self.language,
            "journal_only_dormant": self.journal_only,
            "timeline_strictness": self.timeline_strictness,
            "timeline_trigger_span": self.timeline_trigger_span,
            "raw_directives": list(self.raw_directives),
            "source": self.source,
        }

content.content:
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# I-scope-001 — extensible SOURCE-TYPE / SCOPE facet ontology (Angle A).
#
# The flexible per-question scope+timeline compliance gate reads this file. Facet
# vocabulary is CONFIG, not a Python enum (LAW VI, requirement A "extensible") —
# adding a source-type is ONE YAML block, ZERO Python change.
#
# Each facet carries:
#   id            — the ontology id surfaced in disclosure + the enforcement plan.
#   dimension     — 'source_type' | 'jurisdiction' | 'geography' | 'language' (OPEN set).
#   synonyms      — prompt phrases that resolve to it (the deterministic regex layer
#                   matches these; the LLM fallback resolves unseen phrasings).
#   matchers      — how a SOURCE (not the prompt) is classified into this facet:
#                     hosts        — registrable host suffixes (equal or sub-domain).
#                     url_patterns — lowercased-URL substrings.
#                     doi_prefixes — DOI registrant prefixes (e.g. '10.2138/').
#                     genre        — one of the document_type_classifier genres the
#                                    facet maps to (reuses that classifier, no dup logic).
#                     journal      — true iff the facet IS is_peer_reviewed_journal_article.
#   default_out_of_scope_weight — the (0,1] multiplicative DEMOTE applied to a source
#                                 that is NOT in this facet when the facet is 'prefer'-
#                                 requested at 'weight' strictness. NEVER 0 (demote-not-drop).
#
# The strictness/op lexicons (deontic detection, Angle B) live at the bottom — tunable,
# not hardcoded (§8.3.1 / LAW VI). Every value here is operator-reviewable.
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

facets:
  - id: peer_reviewed_journal
    dimension: source_type
    synonyms:
      - "peer-reviewed journal"
      - "peer reviewed journal"
      - "peer-reviewed article"
      - "journal article"
      - "academic journal"
      - "scholarly journal"
      - "scholarly article"
      - "published journal"
      - "peer-reviewed"
    matchers:
      journal: true
    default_out_of_scope_weight: 0.5

  - id: law_legal
    dimension: source_type
    synonyms:
      - "case law"
      - "statute"
      - "statutes"
      - "legislation"
      - "court decision"
      - "court ruling"
      - "judicial opinion"
      - "legal filing"
      - "legal filings"
      - "regulation text"
      - "legal code"
    matchers:
      hosts:
        - "courtlistener.com"
        - "justia.com"
        - "law.cornell.edu"
        - "supremecourt.gov"
        - "canlii.org"
        - "bailii.org"
        - "eur-lex.europa.eu"
        - "legislation.gov.uk"
        - "laws-lois.justice.gc.ca"
      url_patterns:
        - "/statute"
        - "/case-law"
        - "/opinions/"
    default_out_of_scope_weight: 0.4

  - id: patent
    dimension: source_type
    synonyms:
      - "patent filing"
      - "patent filings"
      - "patent application"
      - "granted patent"
      - "patents"
      - "patent document"
    matchers:
      hosts:
        - "patents.google.com"
        - "patft.uspto.gov"
        - "patentscope.wipo.int"
        - "worldwide.espacenet.com"
        - "espacenet.com"
        - "freepatentsonline.com"
      url_patterns:
        - "/patent/"
        - "patents.google"
    default_out_of_scope_weight: 0.4

  - id: government
    dimension: source_type
    synonyms:
      - "government source"
      - "government sources"
      - "government publication"
      - "government publications"
      - "government report"
      - "official government"
      - "public sector data"
    matchers:
      hosts:
        - "gov"
        - "europa.eu"
        - "who.int"
        - "un.org"
        - "canada.ca"
        - "gov.uk"
      url_patterns:
        - ".gov/"
        - ".gov."
        - ".gc.ca"
    default_out_of_scope_weight: 0.5

content.content:
230	        # MUST be a scope_gate.SUPPORTED_DOMAINS member or run_scope_gate aborts pre-retrieval.
231	        "domain": domain,
232	        "question": task["prompt"],
233	        "amplified": [],
234	        "language": task.get("language", "en"),
235	    }
236	
237	
238	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
239	# GATE stage — compile + pin the artifact
240	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
241	
242	def _run_gate(prompt: str, *, mode: str, live: bool, run_id: str) -> Any:
243	    """Run the REAL research planning gate. Returns a GateResult.
244	
245	    * live=True  -> the gate builds a real OpenRouter policy-model client
246	      (requires PG_PLANNING_GATE_LIVE=1 + OPENROUTER_API_KEY). This is the real-run path.
247	    * live=False -> inject the deterministic OFFLINE compiler stub (spend-free) reused from
248	      the S1 smoke, so the dry smoke exercises the full gate machinery with no network.
249	    """
250	    from src.polaris_graph.planning.research_planning_gate import (
251	        run_research_planning_gate,
252	    )
253	    client = None
254	    if not live:
255	        from scripts.dr_benchmark.gate_s1_smoke import _OfflineStubClient
256	        client = _OfflineStubClient(prompt)
257	    return asyncio.run(
258	        run_research_planning_gate(prompt, mode=mode, client=client, run_id=run_id)
259	    )
260	
261	
262	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
263	# WIRING projections (retrieval / compose-render / audit) — from the pinned artifact
264	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
265	
266	def _retrieval_wiring_proof(artifact: Any, prompt: str) -> dict:
267	    """Compile the RetrievalProjection from the artifact and prove its lanes are populated.
268	
269	    This is the no-starvation wiring the fresh run would consume via PG_GATE=1: the gate's
270	    amplified queries + scope terms + research frame reach FS-Researcher BEFORE any fetch.
271	    """
272	    from src.polaris_graph.planning.retrieval_projection import from_artifact
273	    proj = from_artifact(artifact)
274	    amplified = proj.to_amplified_queries(base_question=prompt)
275	    scope_terms = proj.to_scope_terms()
276	    frame = proj.to_research_frame()
277	    return {
278	        "amplified_query_count": len(amplified),
279	        "amplified_sample": amplified[:5],
280	        "scope_lane_keys": sorted(scope_terms.keys()),
281	        "scope_lane_sizes": {k: len(v) for k, v in scope_terms.items()},
282	        "has_research_frame": frame is not None,
283	        "candidate_query_count": proj.candidate_query_count(base_question=prompt),
284	    }
285	
286	
287	def _compose_render_wiring_proof(artifact: Any) -> dict:
288	    """Compile the ComposeRenderProjection and expose the render plan the compose stage uses."""
289	    from src.polaris_graph.planning.compose_render_projection import from_artifact
290	    crp = from_artifact(artifact)
291	    plan = crp.render_plan()
292	    return {
293	        "document_type": plan.get("document_type", ""),
294	        "required_section_count": len(plan.get("required_titles", []) or []),
295	        "required_titles": plan.get("required_titles", []),
296	        "has_voice": crp.has_voice(),
297	        "voice_advisory_head": (crp.voice_advisory() or "")[:120],
298	    }
299	
300	
301	def _run_audit(artifact: Any, report_text: str, *, retrieval_scope_status: str) -> dict:
302	    """Run the disclosure-only contract-compliance audit over ``report_text``."""
303	    from src.polaris_graph.planning.contract_compliance import audit_contract
304	    audit = audit_contract(
305	        artifact.contract,
306	        report_text,
307	        retrieval_scope_status=retrieval_scope_status,
308	        contract_sha256=getattr(artifact, "contract_sha256", "") or "",
309	    )
310	    return audit.to_dict()
311	
312	
313	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
314	# The ASSEMBLED fresh-e2e pipeline call spec (recorded in dry mode; EXECUTED in live mode)
315	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
316	
317	def _fresh_e2e_env_slate(*, gate_on: bool, mode: str) -> dict:
318	    """The env the fresh e2e stage sets before calling run_one_query.
319	
320	    PG_GATE gates ONLY whether the gate's RetrievalProjection is CONSULTED at the FS seam
321	    (gate_flags.gate_enabled). PG_USE_RESEARCH_PLANNER=1 makes run_one_query build the
322	    _research_plan the S2 hook projects — BOTH are required for the projection to thread (see
323	    run_honest_sweep_r3.py:10448 `_pg_gate_enabled() and _use_research_planner and
324	    _research_plan is not None`). The full-capability retrieval breadth slate (PG_SWEEP_*) is
325	    supplied by the real-run env in the module docstring; we set only the gate switches here.
326	    """
327	    slate = {
328	        "PG_GATE": "1" if gate_on else "0",
329	        "PG_USE_RESEARCH_PLANNER": "1" if gate_on else "0",
330	        "PG_GATE_MODE": mode,
331	    }
332	    return slate
333	
334	
335	def _assembled_pipeline_call(q: dict, out_root: Path, *, gate_on: bool, mode: str) -> dict:
336	    """The exact fresh-e2e call the live run executes. In dry mode this is only RECORDED."""
337	    return {
338	        "entrypoint": "scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3:run_one_query",
339	        "reaches": "S2 gate hook @ run_honest_sweep_r3.py:10435-10468 (DIRECT — no DRB-II lineage)",
